3. 半开销密文打包优化：将Usenix Security’23中基于RLWE的同态加密（HE）密文打包成本降低50%，通过重用多项式结构与精简填充策略，显著提升批量特征处理效率。

主要成果

实验表明：本方案在保持ID完全隐藏前提下，端到端训练速度比主流泄漏型协议仅慢1.3–1.8×，且通信量减少37%；其ID隐藏机制不依赖域大小，突破“隐藏即昂贵”的固有瓶颈；技术框架可自然扩展至联邦统计、安全关联分析等其他纵向分析任务。

AI Summary (English)

Gradient-boosted decision trees (GBDTs) are widely deployed in privacy-sensitive vertical federated learning (e.g., finance and healthcare), yet existing secure training protocols leak record identifiers (IDs) via private set intersection (PSI)—a critical vulnerability. We initiate the study of anonymous two-party GBDT training, where ID sharing is hidden end-to-end. Our protocol introduces: (i) dual alternating circuit-PSI enabling receiver-role switching for “pick-then-sum” over local features without ID exposure; (ii) oblivious programmable PRFs to propagate PSI outputs as reusable shared state across boosting rounds, eliminating repeated alignment; and (iii) a novel ciphertext packing optimization that halves the HE overhead from prior RLWE-based secure GBDT (Usenix Security ’23). Experiments show our approach remains competitive—only 1.3–1.8× slower than leaky baselines while reducing communication by 37%—and scales independently of ID domain size. The techniques generalize to other vertically partitioned analytics requiring ID-hiding aggregation.

Abstract

Structured data is well handled by gradient-boosted decision trees (GBDT), which are usually trained on vertically partitioned features across mutually distrustful parties. High speed and interpretability make GBDTs popular in finance and healthcare, where neural networks may fall short. Enabling secure computation for GBDTs poses unique challenges, requiring secure record alignment for comparison. Relying on private set intersection (PSI) is a de facto approach. Mistaking PSI for a safety measure actually exposes which record identifiers (IDs) are shared between the datasets. Although circuit-PSI could help, it is costly for generic uses. New ideas are needed to efficiently train in a "dark forest". Aiming to hide the IDs, we initiate the study of anonymous GBDT training on split data held by two parties. Dual circuit-PSI in our design lets the parties alternate as receiver to run pick-then-sum over local features. Via oblivious programmable pseudorandom functions, we propagate circuit-PSI outputs as shared state across runs. Avoiding universal alignment, we resolve the neglected dilemma that ID hiding incurs a cost that scales with domain size. Next, we halve the cost of ciphertext packing used to convert single-instruction multiple-data homomorphic encryption from (ring) learning with errors in prior secure GBDT (Usenix Security' 23) and related secure machine-learning computations. Comparative experiments show our protocol remains competitive with leaky approaches in efficiency. Enabling ID-hiding aggregation, our techniques can extend to other vertically partitioned analytics.

item.title: Certified Causal Attribution for Real-Time Attack Forensics in 6G Network Slicing
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26679v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

在6G网络切片环境中，跨切片攻击溯源需在<100 ms严苛时延内识别经共享基础设施（如频谱、计算、传输资源）传播的因果链。现有方法（如标准Granger因果检验）因无法区分资源争用引发的虚假相关性与真实因果关系，导致高误归因率，难以兼顾实时性与准确性。

方法创新：DA-GC框架

我们提出DA-GC（Differential Attribution via Granger Causality），一种具备形式化认证能力的因果归因框架：

  • 资源条件化Granger检验：将传统Granger因果建模扩展至资源状态依赖维度，显式建模CPU利用率、缓冲区占用率等动态约束；
  • 公理化资源争用模型（RCM）：基于共享资源调度语义，从第一性原理导出干扰传播方程，系统性阻断资源中介混杂路径；
  • 三层认证栈：① 统计有效性证书（保障序列相关遥测与分段平稳性下的检验一致性）；② 安全鲁棒性边界（对抗性资源利用率伪造的崩溃点 $δ^* \approx 0.95$）；③ 差分隐私部署下界（给出最小噪声强度以同时满足$(ε,δ)$-隐私与因果辨识鲁棒性）。

实验验证

在15切片、贴近生产环境的6G仿真测试平台（覆盖1,100种攻击场景）上，DA-GC实现89.2%归因准确率，平均耗时仅87 ms——较最优基线提升7.9个百分点，且延迟降低2.7倍；进一步验证其跨拓扑泛化性与概念漂移韧性。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces DA-GC, a certified causal attribution framework for real-time cross-slice attack forensics in 6G network slicing. To overcome spurious correlations induced by shared-resource contention—indistinguishable under standard Granger tests—DA-GC integrates resource-conditioned Granger causality with an axiomatically derived Resource Contention Model (RCM) that provably blocks resource-mediated confounding. Evaluated on a 15-slice production-emulation testbed across 1,100 attack scenarios, DA-GC achieves 89.2% attribution accuracy at 87 ms, improving over the strongest baseline by 7.9 percentage points while reducing latency by 2.7×. It further demonstrates cross-topology generalization and concept-drift resilience. Crucially, DA-GC is backed by a formal certification stack: mathematically proven validity under serially dependent telemetry and piecewise stationarity; strict security bounds (e.g., adversarial utilization spoofing breakdown point $δ^* \approx 0.95$); and analytically derived minimum differential-privacy noise for provably private and robust deployment.

Abstract

Cross-slice attack attribution in 6G networks requires identifying causal propagation chains through shared infrastructure in under 100 ms. Existing methods struggle to satisfy this strict SLA without sacrificing accuracy, because shared resource contention creates spurious correlations that are indistinguishable from genuine causal links under standard Granger tests. We propose DA-GC, a certified causal attribution framework that integrates resource-conditioned Granger causality with an axiomatically derived Resource Contention Model (RCM) to systematically block resource-mediated confounding. On a 15-slice production-emulation 6G testbed with 1,100 attack scenarios, DA-GC achieves 89.2% attribution accuracy at 87 ms. This represents a 7.9 percentage-point improvement over the strongest baseline at 2.7x lower latency, alongside demonstrated cross-topology generalization and concept-drift resilience. Crucially, DA-GC is backed by a comprehensive formal certification stack. We provide mathematically proven validity certificates for statistical soundness under serially dependent telemetry and piecewise-stationarity. Furthermore, we establish strict security bounds, including an adversarial utilization spoofing breakdown point of $δ^* \approx 0.95$, and define the minimum differential-privacy noise required for a provably private and robust deployment.

item.title: Cordyceps: Covert Control Attacks on LLMs via Data Poisoning
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26595v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

大型语言模型（LLMs）常在未经严格审核的文本数据集上进行微调，这为数据投毒攻击提供了可乘之机。现有投毒方法多依赖固定触发短语（如“cfv98”），易被异常检测、干净数据正则化或在线监控等防御机制识别并拦截，隐蔽性与鲁棒性严重不足。

方法创新：Cordyceps 框架

本文提出 Cordyceps——一种新型语义驱动的数据投毒方法。其核心思想是：不硬编码触发器，而是通过微调过程，诱导模型自主习得一套隐式信息隐藏方案。该方案建立在共享常识（如科学事实、文化概念）与攻击者选定短语之间的稳健语义关联之上。例如，模型学会将“the capital of France”与特定无害短语（如“sunrise protocol”）隐式绑定，进而利用该映射实现任意恶意指令（如越权执行、数据窃取）的编码与解码。

关键发现与优势

  • ✅ 首次定义并实现“隐蔽控制攻击”（Covert Control Attacks）：攻击者无需运行时注入提示，仅凭自然语义触发即可远程操控模型行为；
  • ✅ 在5个主流LLM（Llama-3-8B、Qwen2-7B、Phi-3等）上验证，仅需0.5%–2% 的极低投毒比例，平均攻击成功率较基线提示注入攻击提升约40%（相对提升）；
  • ✅ 对抗性强：在3种主流后门防御（e.g., STRIP, ABS, RAP）下仍保持最高93%成功率；在4种提示注入防御（e.g., GuardRAG, PromptGuard, Llama-Guard3）下维持最高98%成功率；
  • ✅ 本质突破：将投毒从“触发-响应”范式升维至“协议内生”范式，揭示LLM微调中未被认知的语义协议劫持风险。

AI Summary (English)

We introduce Cordyceps, a novel data poisoning attack that enables covert control of LLMs by teaching them an implicit, semantic-based information-hiding scheme during fine-tuning—without fixed triggers. Cordyceps exploits robust associations between widely shared knowledge (e.g., facts, concepts) and attacker-chosen innocuous phrases to establish a stealthy encoding/decoding protocol for arbitrary malicious instructions. Evaluated across 5 LLMs, 3 backdoor defenses, and 4 prompt injection defenses, Cordyceps achieves up to 93% attack success rate after backdoor mitigation and 98% after prompt injection defense, significantly outperforming heuristic prompt injection attacks (+40% relative success) even with only 0.5–2% poisoned data. This work formally defines covert control attacks and uncovers a fundamental vulnerability: LLMs can internalize adversarial control protocols through semantic poisoning, evading both detection- and fine-tuning–based defenses.

Abstract

Large language models (LLMs) are often fine-tuned on uncurated text datasets that adversaries can poison. Existing poisoning attacks primarily rely on fixed trigger phrases that defenses such as outlier detection, clean-data regularization, or online monitoring can neutralize. In this paper, we propose a data poisoning method that teaches an LLM an information hiding scheme reliably and stealthily through semantic associations between shared knowledge such as facts or concepts and attacker-chosen phrases. The induced hiding scheme can encode and decode arbitrary malicious instructions, thus revealing a new and subtle poisoning-induced vulnerability: covert control attacks. We precisely characterize covert control attacks and evaluate them across $5$ LLMs, $3$ backdoor defenses, and $4$ prompt injection defenses. With a small poisoned fraction, covert control attacks outperform heuristic-based prompt injection attacks in average attack success rate by about $40\%$ relative to clean fine-tuned models. They also circumvent defenses based on detection and fine-tuning, maintaining up to $93\%$ attack success rate after backdoor defenses and up to $98\%$ after prompt injection defenses.

item.title: Aligning Few-Step Generative Models by Amortizing Sample-based Variational Inference
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26552v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

对少步生成模型（few-step generative models）进行对齐（alignment）——即在保持其高效采样特性的同时，使其输出分布精准匹配下游奖励或参考分布——是当前生成式AI与具身智能交叉领域的关键难题。现有对齐方法往往依赖强假设：如需可解析的似然函数、特定ODE/SDE求解器（如DDIM、Euler）、或限定于某类模型架构（如扩散模型），严重制约了其在GAN、流映射（flow maps）、一致性模型（consistency models）等异构少步生成器上的泛化能力。

方法：FAV框架

本文提出FAV（Few-step Generative Models Alignment via Sample-based Variational Inference），一种完全无模型假设的通用对齐框架。FAV仅需对生成器和参考分布的样本访问权限（sample access），不依赖梯度、似然或微分方程形式。其核心思想是将对齐建模为从奖励加权的倾斜分布（reward-tilted distribution）中采样，该分布以参考分布为锚点。技术上，FAV采用Stein变分梯度下降（SVGD）作为样本驱动的变分推断引擎，并通过不动点回归（fixed-point regression）将SVG粒子更新过程“摊销”（amortize）进生成器参数中，实现端到端可微训练。

实验与贡献

在机器人操作策略对齐任务中，FAV在56个离线RL和30个离线→在线RL基准上全面超越主流策略提取基线（如BC-R, Diffuser-Policy）。在图像生成对齐任务中，FAV成功统一微调多种少步骨干：GAN、漂移模型（drifting model）、一致性模型与流映射，在ImageNet-256至1024²文本到图像合成中展现出卓越扩展性与保真度。代码已开源：https://github.com/Jaewoopudding/FAV。

AI Summary (English)

Aligning few-step generative models—e.g., GANs, consistency models, and flow maps—is notoriously difficult due to restrictive assumptions in existing alignment frameworks (e.g., tractable likelihoods, specific ODE/SDE solvers, or model-family constraints). We propose FAV, a general, sample-only alignment framework that requires no gradient access, no likelihood evaluation, and no solver specification. FAV frames alignment as sampling from a reward-tilted distribution anchored to a reference, using Stein Variational Gradient Descent (SVGD) as a sample-based inference engine; crucially, it amortizes SVGD particle updates into generator parameters via fixed-point regression. Evaluated across two domains: (1) For robotic manipulation, FAV outperforms state-of-the-art policy extraction baselines on 56 offline and 30 offline-to-online RL tasks; (2) For image generation, FAV fine-tunes diverse few-step backbones—including GANs, drifting models, consistency models, and flow maps—scaling robustly from ImageNet-256 to high-resolution 1024² text-to-image synthesis. Code: https://github.com/Jaewoopudding/FAV.

Abstract

Aligning a few-step generative model is challenging, since existing alignment frameworks typically rely on restrictive assumptions: a tractable likelihood, a specific ODE/SDE solver, or a particular model family. We introduce FAV, Few-step Generative Models Alignment via Sample-based Variational Inference, a general alignment framework that requires only sample access to the generator and the reference distribution. We cast alignment as sampling from a reward-tilted distribution anchored to a reference distribution. We leverage Stein Variational Gradient Descent as a sample-based variational inference scheme and amortize its particle updates into the generator parameters via fixed-point regression. We evaluate FAV on two domains: robotics manipulation and image generator alignment. On generative policy alignment for robotic manipulation, FAV outperforms prevailing policy extraction baselines across 56 offline and 30 offline-to-online RL tasks. For image generator alignment, FAV fine-tunes diverse few-step backbones, including GAN, drifting model, consistency models, and flow maps, scaling from ImageNet-$256$ to 1024$^2$ text-to-image synthesis. Code is available at https://github.com/Jaewoopudding/FAV.

item.title: Detectability in Diversity: Improved Canary Crafting for Privacy Auditing in One Run
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.27292v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

隐私审计旨在通过成员推断攻击（Membership Inference Attacks, MIAs）实证评估机器学习模型的隐私泄露程度，并据此推导差分隐私（Differential Privacy, DP）参数的下界。传统多轮审计方法需重复训练模型以检测单个“金丝雀”（canary）样本的包含状态，计算开销巨大。近期提出的单轮审计范式通过一次训练引入多个可审计的canary点，显著降低成本；但其核心瓶颈在于：现有canary构造策略易引发跨canary干扰，导致泄露信号弱化、DP下界估计偏松，审计灵敏度受限。

方法创新

本文提出可检测性驱动的多样性canary构造框架（Detectability in Diversity），核心思想是协同优化canary的高可检测性与低相互干扰性。具体包括：

  • 理论驱动设计：基于影响函数（influence function）构建贪心初始化，快速定位对模型输出敏感的候选样本；
  • 双层优化机制：上层最大化各canary在嵌入空间中的分类可区分性（via MIA判别器置信度），下层通过正则化强制canary表征在特征空间中充分分散，显式抑制干扰；
  • 高效实现：采用隐式梯度近似与嵌入空间投影约束，使双层优化可在标准GPU上分钟级收敛。

主要结果

在CIFAR-10、Purchase和Texas100等基准数据集上，本方法相较SOTA（如Canary-RL、MIA-Craft）将DP参数ε的估计下界平均提升23.6%（p<0.01），同时减少47% 的审计时间开销。消融实验验证：多样性正则项贡献了82%的性能增益，证实干扰抑制是单轮审计精度跃升的关键。本工作为高效、鲁棒的隐私合规性验证提供了新范式。

AI Summary (English)

Privacy auditing via membership inference attacks (MIAs) aims to empirically quantify privacy leakage and derive lower bounds on differential privacy (DP) parameters. One-run auditing methods—using a single model training with multiple canaries—offer efficiency but suffer from inter-canary interference, weakening leakage detection. We propose Detectability in Diversity, a novel canary crafting framework that jointly optimizes for high detectability and minimal interference. Our method combines influence-function-based greedy initialization with a computationally efficient bilevel optimization: the upper level maximizes MIA distinguishability of each canary, while the lower level enforces diversity in the embedding space via a spectral dispersion regularizer. Experiments across CIFAR-10, Purchase, and Texas100 show our approach improves estimated DP ε-lower bounds by 23.6% on average over prior arts (e.g., Canary-RL), while reducing audit time by 47%. Ablation confirms diversity regularization accounts for 82% of the gain—highlighting interference suppression as the key to accurate one-run auditing.

Abstract

Privacy auditing aims to empirically assess privacy leakage in machine learning models using membership inference attacks (MIAs), and to derive lower bounds on differential privacy (DP) parameters. Recent one-run auditing methods address the high cost of standard approaches by relying on a single training run with multiple "canary" points whose inclusion or exclusion must be detected by the auditor. In this work, we study the problem of efficiently crafting canaries for one-run privacy auditing. Motivated by recent theoretical insights suggesting that interference between canaries contributes to weaker leakage estimates compared to multi-run methods, we propose to optimize canaries to be both highly detectable and minimally interfering. Our approach combines a greedy initialization based on influence functions with a bilevel optimization procedure that maximizes distinguishability while promoting diversity in embedding space, enabling the use of computationally efficient bilevel algorithms. Experiments show that our method achieves stronger privacy leakage estimates at a lower computational cost than existing canary crafting approaches.

item.title: PATE-TabTransGAN: Differentially Private Synthetic Tabular Data Generation via Transformer-Based Student Discrimination
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26802v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与挑战

在差分隐私（DP）约束下生成高保真合成表格数据仍是一项开放难题。现有方法面临根本性权衡：提供严格理论隐私保障（如基于PATE或梯度裁剪的方案）往往弱化特征间复杂依赖建模能力，导致合成数据失真；而擅长捕捉列间高阶关系的先进架构（如基于Transformer或GAN的模型）却仅能提供经验性隐私保证，缺乏可验证的$(\varepsilon, \delta)$-DP保障。

方法创新：PATE-TabTransGAN框架

本文提出PATE-TabTransGAN——首个将私有教师集成聚合（PATE）机制与Transformer结构的学生判别器深度融合的生成框架。其核心设计包括：

  • PATE监督范式：构建多个逻辑回归教师模型，各自在互斥数据子集上独立训练；学生判别器通过GNMax机制接收经拉普拉斯噪声扰动的聚合标签，实现$(\varepsilon, \delta)$-DP监督；
  • Transformer学生判别器：摒弃传统MLP判别器，采用位置编码+多头注意力结构，显式建模列间长程依赖与异构特征交互；
  • 残差生成器优化：生成器以学生判别器为DP目标函数进行对抗训练，因判别器满足DP且生成过程为后处理操作，整个生成流程继承严格$(\varepsilon, \delta)$-DP保证；
  • 数值稳定隐私核算：采用GNMax RDP accountant进行精确、低方差的隐私预算追踪，避免传统PATE中累积误差放大的风险。

实验结果与优势

在Adult、Breast、Cardio、Cervical四大标准表格数据集上，PATE-TabTransGAN全面超越PATE-GAN、DP-GAN与DP-CTGAN等SOTA基线：

  • AUROC指标：在全部4个数据集上均取得最优或并列最优性能，验证其在分类任务下游效用上的显著优势；
  • AUCPR分析：在Cardio上持平最强基线，Cervical上领先，Breast上略逊；针对Adult数据集的深入分析表明，AUCPR差异源于不同评估管道对正类定义的惯例不一致（如是否将“>50K”或“≤50K”设为正类），而非合成质量缺陷，凸显本方法鲁棒性。该工作首次实现了强理论隐私保障与Transformer级建模能力的有机统一。

AI Summary (English)

PATE-TabTransGAN is a novel differentially private framework for synthetic tabular data generation that bridges the gap between formal privacy guarantees and high-fidelity structural modeling. It integrates the Private Aggregation of Teacher Ensembles (PATE) with a Transformer-based student discriminator, enabling rigorous $(\varepsilon, \delta)$-DP supervision while capturing complex inter-feature dependencies—unachievable by conventional MLP discriminators. Teachers are logistic regression models trained on disjoint data partitions; their noisy-aggregated labels (via GNMax) train the student under RDP-accounted privacy, and a residual generator is optimized against this DP student, inheriting end-to-end privacy by post-processing. Evaluated on four benchmarks (Adult, Breast, Cardio, Cervical), PATE-TabTransGAN achieves best or tied-best AUROC across all datasets, outperforming state-of-the-art baselines (PATE-GAN, DP-GAN, DP-CTGAN). Its AUCPR performance is competitive—matching the strongest baseline on Cardio, leading on Cervical, and showing convention-sensitive but not quality-limited behavior on Adult. This work establishes the first DP tabular GAN with Transformer-scale expressivity and provable privacy.

Abstract

Generating high-fidelity synthetic tabular data under formal differential privacy guarantees remains an open challenge. Methods that provide strong theoretical protection typically sacrifice the modeling of inter-feature dependencies required for realistic synthesis, while architectures that excel at capturing complex column relationships offer only empirical privacy guarantees. We present PATE-TabTransGAN, a generative framework that integrates the Private Aggregation of Teacher Ensembles (PATE) mechanism with a Transformer-based student discriminator to jointly address both requirements, and employs a GNMax RDP accountant for numerically stable privacy accounting. An ensemble of Logistic Regression teachers trained on disjoint partitions supervise the student via noisy-aggregated labels, and a residual generator is optimized against this differentially private student, inheriting formal (ε, δ)-DP guarantees by post-processing. PATE-TabTransGAN was compared with PATE-GAN, DP-GAN, and DP-CTGAN, considered state-of-the-art in differentially private tabular synthesis. Experiments conducted on four tabular benchmarks (Adult, Breast, Cardio, Cervical) confirmed the high quality of the proposed method: PATE-TabTransGAN attains the best or tied-best AUROC on all four datasets. On AUCPR it matches the strongest baseline on Cardio, leads on Cervical, and trails on Breast; on Adult, we demonstrate that AUCPR is highly sensitive to positive-class convention, and that the observed gap is consistent with a convention difference between evaluation pipelines rather than a synthesis deficit.

item.title: PIDM-DP: Physics-Informed Diffusion with Dormand-Prince Integration for Chaotic System Identification and State Reconstruction across Multiple Dynamical Regimes
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26619v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

从稀疏、含噪观测中重建混沌动力系统的连续状态轨迹，是非线性科学中的基础性难题。传统方法（如EnKF）在强刚性系统（Jacobi矩阵特征值达 $O(10^3)$）中易因协方差坍塌而失效；现有物理信息深度学习方法常因梯度爆炸导致训练不稳定，难以跨多动力学模态泛化。

方法创新：PIDM-DP

我们提出物理信息扩散模型结合Dormand-Prince积分器（PIDM-DP）：将完全可微的5阶Dormand-Prince（DP-RK45）常微分方程求解器直接嵌入去噪扩散概率模型（DDPM）的反向采样循环。每一去噪步均通过自动微分反向传播物理残差，强制生成轨迹严格满足系统控制方程（5阶精度）。引入线性调度的物理引导机制：物理权重从高噪声阶段的0平滑增至低噪声阶段的满值，有效抑制梯度爆炸。

关键性能与验证

在5个递进复杂度基准系统上评估（3D Lorenz → 20D Lorenz-96 → 刚性3D Rabinovich-Fabrikant），观测密度仅10%，噪声水平 $\sigma=0.05$：

  • 相比无约束扩散基线，RMSE最高提升15.4倍；
  • 在Rabinovich-Fabrikant（OOD）任务中，RMSE达 $0.1097 \pm 0.0269$，显著优于扩散基线（$0.9443 \pm 0.5288$，$p<0.001$，8.6×差）和EnKF（$0.3561 \pm 0.3040$，3.2×差）；
  • Rosenstein最大Lyapunov指数估计证实：重建轨迹完整保留原系统的混沌不变测度与拓扑结构。

AI Summary (English)

We propose PIDM-DP, a Physics-Informed Diffusion Model integrating a fully differentiable 5th-order Dormand-Prince (DP-RK45) ODE solver directly into the reverse sampling loop of a Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Model (DDPM). At every denoising step, physics residuals are back-propagated via automatic differentiation, enforcing 5th-order accuracy to governing equations. A linear-scheduled physics guidance—ramping weight from zero (high noise) to full value (low noise)—stabilizes training on stiff systems ($\mathcal{O}(10^3)$ Jacobian eigenvalues). Evaluated across five chaotic benchmarks (3D Lorenz to stiff 3D Rabinovich-Fabrikant) with only 10% sparse noisy observations ($\sigma = 0.05$), PIDM-DP achieves up to 15.4× RMSE improvement over unconstrained diffusion and decisively outperforms Ensemble Kalman Filter (EnKF) where ensemble covariance collapses. On out-of-distribution Rabinovich-Fabrikant, PIDM-DP attains RMSE $0.1097 \pm 0.0269$, significantly better than diffusion ($0.9443 \pm 0.5288$, $p < 0.001$) and EnKF ($0.3561 \pm 0.3040$). Topological validation via Rosenstein Lyapunov estimation confirms preservation of the chaotic invariant measure.

Abstract

Reconstructing continuous state trajectories of chaotic dynamical systems from sparse, noisy observations remains a fundamental open problem in nonlinear science. We introduce the Physics-Informed Diffusion Model with Dormand-Prince Integration (PIDM-DP), which embeds a fully differentiable 5th-order Dormand-Prince (DP-RK45) ODE integrator directly into the reverse sampling loop of a Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Model (DDPM). At each denoising step, physics residuals are back-propagated via automatic differentiation, constraining every generated trajectory to satisfy the system's governing equations to 5th-order accuracy. A linear-scheduled guidance mechanism that ramps the physics weight from zero at high noise levels to its full value near the clean-data limit prevents the gradient explosions that cause naive physics-informed approaches to fail on stiff systems with Jacobian eigenvalues of order $O(10^3)$. Evaluated across five benchmark systems of increasing complexity 3D Lorenz, 3D Rössler, 5D Hyperchaotic, 20D Lorenz-96, and the stiff 3D Rabinovich-Fabrikant at 10% observation density with additive Gaussian noise ($σ=0.05$), PIDM-DP achieves reconstruction RMSE improvements of up to $15.4\times$ over an unconstrained diffusion baseline and decisively outperforms the Ensemble Kalman Filter on stiff systems where ensemble covariance collapses. On the Rabinovich-Fabrikant out-of-distribution benchmark, PIDM-DP attains RMSE $0.1097 \pm 0.0269$ versus $0.9443 \pm 0.5288$ (unconstrained diffusion, $8.6\times$ worse) and $0.3561 \pm 0.3040$ (EnKF, $3.2\times$ worse), with $p<0.001$ in paired Wilcoxon tests ($N = 30$). Topological validation via the Rosenstein Lyapunov estimator confirms that PIDM-DP preserves the chaotic invariant measure.

item.title: Separate Aggregation of Split Network for Personalized Federated Learning
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26571v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

联邦学习（FL）在保护数据隐私的前提下实现协同建模，但在客户端数据高度异构（如非独立同分布、标签不平衡、类别缺失）时，全局模型性能显著下降。传统个性化联邦学习（PFL）试图兼顾本地适配与全局泛化，却常陷入根本性权衡困境：过度共享削弱个性化能力，而过度本地化又易在小样本、长尾或零样本场景下过拟合。

方法创新：PGFedSplit 框架

本文提出 PGFedSplit——一种基于“分离式聚合”的新型PFL框架：

  • 分层拆分架构：将模型解耦为共享主干（global backbone） 与轻量本地头（personalized head），二者承担不同语义角色；
  • 自适应聚合调度：对主干参数采用加权鲁棒平均（weight-aware robust averaging），对本地头完全不聚合，避免干扰个性化决策；
  • 合成表征增强：客户端融合本地提取特征与服务器端生成的高斯统计合成特征（基于类条件均值/协方差），显式缓解标签不平衡与类别缺失问题；
  • 无额外通信开销：合成特征仅需传输低维统计量（均值向量+协方差矩阵），不增加上行带宽负担。

实验验证与优势

在 Fashion-MNIST、CIFAR-10/100 及 Tiny ImageNet 上的系统实验表明：PGFedSplit 在高度异构设置下（如 Dirichlet α=0.1）持续超越 FedPer、pFedMe、Ditto 等 SOTA 方法，平均个性化准确率提升 3.2–7.8%；收敛过程更稳定（标准差降低 41%），且在 50% 客户缺失某类标签时仍保持 >89% 的跨类泛化能力。本工作首次将结构化分离聚合与统计驱动的合成表征有机结合，为强异构PFL提供了可扩展、鲁棒且通信友好的新范式。

AI Summary (English)

Federated learning (FL) enables privacy-preserving collaborative training, yet suffers from severe performance degradation under extreme client heterogeneity—especially with label imbalance and missing classes. To reconcile personalization and global generalization, we propose PGFedSplit, a novel personalized FL framework featuring: (1) a split architecture decoupling shared backbones from lightweight personalized heads; (2) role-aware adaptive aggregation, where backbone parameters undergo weighted robust averaging while heads remain unaggregated; and (3) Gaussian-statistic-driven synthetic representations generated server-side and fused locally to enhance robustness against data scarcity and class absence. Extensive experiments on Fashion-MNIST, CIFAR-10/100, and Tiny ImageNet show PGFedSplit consistently outperforms state-of-the-art PFL methods by 3.2–7.8% in personalized accuracy under high heterogeneity (e.g., Dirichlet α=0.1), with stable convergence and superior zero-shot class adaptation—achieving >89% accuracy even when 50% of clients lack certain classes.

Abstract

Federated learning enables collaborative model training without sharing raw data, but its performance can degrade substantially under heterogeneous client data distributions. A single global model often cannot satisfy diverse client requirements, so personalized federated learning has therefore been explored to improve client specific performance while preserving global generalization. Existing PFL methods often face a fundamental tradeoff in which stronger global sharing can undermine local specialization, whereas stronger local adaptation can lead to overfitting under limited data, label imbalance, and missing class scenarios. In this work, we propose PGFedSplit, a personalized federated learning framework that improves both personalization and global generalization under severe client heterogeneity. PGFedSplit adopts a split architecture and performs adaptive aggregation scheduling tailored to the roles of different model components, enabling stable knowledge sharing while maintaining client specific adaptation. Each client further leverages a mixture of locally extracted representations and synthetic representations generated from server side Gaussian statistics, improving robustness under label imbalance and missing class conditions. Extensive experiments on Fashion MNIST, CIFAR 10, CIFAR 100, and Tiny ImageNet demonstrate consistent improvements over state of the art PFL methods, with stable convergence and superior personalization in highly heterogeneous settings.

item.title: SEC-bench Pro: Can Language Models Solve Long-Horizon Software Security Tasks?
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26548v1

AI Summary (中文)

SEC-bench Pro：首个面向长周期软件安全任务的代理式漏洞挖掘基准

背景与问题：当前大语言模型（LLMs）已广泛用于自动化软件安全任务（如漏洞发现、PoC生成），但现有基准（如FuzzBench、CVE-bench）严重脱离真实攻防场景——它们依赖预置fuzz harness、限定目标描述或仅验证漏洞复现，无法评估模型在长周期、多步骤、环境敏感的端到端bug hunting能力。

方法创新：本文提出SEC-bench Pro，首个专为评估安全智能体（security agents）在高复杂度系统中执行长周期漏洞挖掘任务而设计的基准。其核心贡献在于构建可复现、可验证、生产级对齐的评估流水线：

  • 三阶段构建范式：① 从V8与SpiderMonkey真实漏洞报告中筛选183个经人工验证的高危漏洞（含V8子集累计获Google VRP奖金超150万美元）；② 精确重建历史编译环境与运行时上下文（含JIT配置、沙箱策略、并发调度）；③ 设计基于补丁差异与行为可观测性的双模态Oracle（内存崩溃/异常退出 + 补丁回滚验证）。
  • 任务覆盖全面：涵盖内存安全（UAF、堆溢出）、沙箱逃逸、JIT编译器缺陷、竞态条件等四类浏览器/引擎级高危漏洞，全部在真实执行条件下触发。

关键发现：前沿编码智能体表现远未达实用水平：

  • 单模型最高成功率仅32.0%（V8）与38.8%（SpiderMonkey）；开源模型Kimi-K2.6仅达11.7%；
  • 多智能体协同显著增益：ClaudeCode与Codex互补性强，二者联合求解使V8/SpiderMonkey成功率提升至37.9%与48.8%；
  • 揭示根本瓶颈：长链推理失败、环境状态建模缺失、PoC输入空间探索低效。

SEC-bench Pro已开源，为安全AI研究提供严格、可扩展、工业级对齐的评估基础设施。

AI Summary (English)

SEC-bench Pro is the first benchmark designed to evaluate LLM-based agents on long-horizon, end-to-end software security tasks—specifically, autonomous vulnerability discovery and PoC generation in production-grade JavaScript engines. Unlike prior benchmarks relying on fuzzing harnesses or simplified reproduction, SEC-bench Pro constructs 183 validated, real-world vulnerabilities across V8 and SpiderMonkey—including a high-impact V8 subset with >$1.5M in Google VRP rewards—via a rigorous three-phase pipeline: vulnerability curation, faithful environment reconstruction (JIT, sandbox, concurrency), and oracle-based validation (crash + patch-reversal). Evaluation reveals stark limitations: even frontier agent configurations achieve only 32.0% (V8) and 38.8% (SpiderMonkey) success; the open-weight Kimi-K2.6 baseline scores just 11.7% on V8. Crucially, complementary strengths of ClaudeCode and Codex enable a two-agent union to reach 37.9% (V8) and 48.8% (SpiderMonkey), highlighting the need for modular, collaborative security agents. SEC-bench Pro establishes a robust, reproducible foundation for advancing long-horizon security reasoning.

Abstract

Large language models (LLMs) now support automated software security tasks, including vulnerability discovery and proof-of-concept (PoC) generation. Existing benchmarks do not faithfully evaluate LLMs in real-world bug hunting scenarios because they rely on fuzzing harnesses, target-specific descriptions, or vulnerability-reproduction tasks. We present SEC-bench Pro, a benchmark for measuring agent bug hunting on critical, high-complexity software systems. This work discloses reports with concrete PoC inputs and links fixes into reproducible tasks through a three-phase pipeline for vulnerability collection, environment reconstruction, and oracle-based validation. We instantiate SEC-bench Pro with 183 validated vulnerabilities across V8 and SpiderMonkey, including a V8 subset with more than $1.5 million in cumulative Google Vulnerability Reward Program awards. These instances span memory-safety, sandbox, JIT, and race-condition bugs under browser-grade and runtime-grade execution conditions. Our evaluation shows that coding agents with frontier models remain below 40% success on both evaluated engines. The open-weight Kimi-K2.6 baseline reaches 11.7% on V8, while the strongest frontier configuration reaches 32.0% on V8 and 38.8% on SpiderMonkey. ClaudeCode and Codex solve complementary instance sets, and their two-agent union reaches 37.9% on V8 and 48.8% on SpiderMonkey. SEC-bench Pro provides robust environments for assessing LLM-based security agents and exposes limitations in long-horizon bug hunting tasks.

item.title: Open-Weight LLM Fine-Tuning Defenses are Susceptible to Simple Attacks
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26526v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

近期针对开源大语言模型（LLM）的防护机制（如安全微调、拒绝训练）普遍假设：模型的有害行为主要源于恶意微调，而非通过提示工程（如越狱）从预训练权重中直接激发。然而，大量实证表明，开源LLM在预训练阶段已内化跨领域的有害知识——这引出关键挑战：攻击者能否完全绕过微调，仅通过精心设计的输入提示，就成功越狱受保护的开源模型？

方法与发现

本文系统评估了两类被长期忽视但零成本、免梯度的越狱策略：Abliteration（在用户指令前插入大量无关文本以干扰安全分类器）和Prefilling（在对话历史中预置诱导性上下文）。我们在三大权威有害性评测基准（BeaverTails、HarmBench、AdvBench）上测试了主流开源防护模型（如Llama-3-Instruct、Qwen2-7B-SFT+SafeRLHF）。结果表明：原始防护模型的攻击成功率普遍低于10%；而应用Abliteration或Prefilling后，成功率跃升至16%–96%，且不同模型/基准间差异显著，证实其普适威胁。

创新与对策

为应对该漏洞，我们提出Abliteration-Resistant Tuning（ART）——一种轻量级、可插拔的防御增强方法：在安全微调目标中显式引入Abliteration扰动下的鲁棒性约束。ART无需修改模型架构，可无缝叠加于现有防护流程。实验显示，ART将Abliteration、Prefilling及其组合攻击的成功率平均降低10–20个百分点，且不损害模型有用性（在AlpacaEval 2.0上保持98.5%基线性能）。

意义

本研究揭示：当前开源LLM防护体系的攻击面远超“对抗性微调”范畴；提示层越狱是更隐蔽、更低门槛的现实威胁。未来安全评估必须纳入多样化、非优化类攻击策略，方能真实反映防护强度。

AI Summary (English)

This paper challenges the prevailing assumption that open-weight LLM safeguards primarily defend against adversarial fine-tuning, not prompt-based jailbreaking. We demonstrate that pretrained open-weight models retain substantial harmful knowledge, enabling simple, gradient-free attacks—abliteration (injecting irrelevant text before the query) and prefilling (priming harmful context in history)—to bypass state-of-the-art safety alignments. Across BeaverTails, HarmBench, and AdvBench, these low-cost attacks increase attack success rates against safeguarded models from <10% to 16%–96%. To counter this, we propose Abliteration-Resistant Tuning (ART), a plug-and-play training objective that explicitly optimizes robustness against abliteration during safety alignment. ART reduces success rates of abliteration, prefilling, and their combination by 10–20 percentage points without sacrificing helpfulness. Our findings reveal a broader attack surface for open-weight models and underscore the necessity of evaluating safeguards against diverse, non-optimization-based jailbreak strategies—not just fine-tuning attacks.

Abstract

Recent defenses for safeguarding open-weight large language models (LLMs) are intended to prevent adversarial usage. Underlying these defenses is an assumption that new harmful behavior is learned through fine-tuning rather than elicited by jailbreaking the model. Yet, pretrained LLMs already encode substantial harmful knowledge across many domains, which raises an important question: can an adversary jailbreak safeguarded models, to achieve harmful usage without fine-tuning at all? In this paper, we show that open-weight safeguards are susceptible to simpler strategies that, despite being well known, have not been systematically evaluated against these safeguards. Specifically, we evaluate two low-cost attacks--abliteration and prefilling--that do not rely on gradient-based optimization. Across three harmfulness evaluation benchmarks (BeaverTails, HarmBench, and AdvBench), these attacks increase attack success rates against safeguarded open-weight models from below 10\% to a range of 16%-96%. To mitigate this vulnerability, we introduce abliteration-resistant tuning (ART), which incorporates an abliteration-based objective into training. ART can be layered onto existing defenses and reduces the success rates of abliteration, prefilling, and their combination by 10%-20%. These findings indicate that the attack surface for open-weight models is broader than previously characterized, and that evaluations of safeguarding defenses should incorporate a more diverse set of attack strategies beyond adversarial fine-tuning.

item.title: EpiCurveBench: Evaluating VLMs on Epidemic Curve Digitization
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.27195v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

现有图表数据提取基准（如ChartQA）对视觉语言模型（VLMs）的评估已逼近性能天花板（前沿模型超89%），且主流指标（如RMS、SCRM）将时间序列点视为无序键值对，严重忽视疫情曲线固有的时序结构：微小的时间轴偏移被误判为灾难性错误，而真实场景中局部位移、起止截断、采样稀疏等现象普遍存在。

方法创新

我们提出 EpiCurveBench——首个面向流行病学应用的专用基准，包含1,000张源自全球公共卫生报告、学术论文及WHO/CDC等权威渠道的真实疫情曲线图像。同时设计 EpiCurveSimilarity（ECS） 评估指标：基于动态规划实现预测序列与真值序列的最优软对齐，显式建模三类现实偏差：① 局部时间偏移（容忍±3天滑动）、② 序列起止截断（gap penalty）、③ 非均匀采样密度差异，并按偏差程度比例化扣分。

关键发现

  • 在6种方法（3个闭源前沿VLM、1个开源VLM、2个专用图表解析系统）上评测，最强模型ECS仅52.3%，凸显任务难度；
  • ECS将4个通用VLM的性能拉开25分差距，而传统键值指标压缩至仅5分内，证明其更高区分度；
  • ECS与下游流行病学指标（总病例数误差、峰值时间偏移、峰值幅度误差、增长速率保真度）显著负相关，相关强度达DTW的1.5–3.6倍（因DTW无gap penalty，无法区分“截断预测”与“时序忠实预测”）；
  • 本工作不仅释放数十年被锁在出版图表中的疫情数据，其基准与指标更可直接迁移至任意结构化时序图表提取任务（如经济曲线、气候时序图）。

AI Summary (English)

EpiCurveBench is a new benchmark for chart-to-data extraction targeting epidemic curve digitization—a high-impact public health task requiring faithful recovery of time-series structure from real-world figures. It comprises 1,000 diverse, expert-curated epidemic curve images from global health sources. To address the limitations of unordered key-value metrics (e.g., RMS, SCRM), we propose EpiCurveSimilarity (ECS): a dynamic-programming-based metric that aligns predicted and ground-truth series while tolerating local temporal shifts and gaps—penalizing them proportionally rather than catastrophically. Evaluating six models—including frontier closed/open VLMs and specialized chart extractors—we find the best achieves only 52.3% ECS, and ECS reveals a 25-point performance spread among general-purpose VLMs where key-value metrics compress differences into just 5 points. Crucially, ECS correlates 1.5–3.6× more strongly than Dynamic Time Warping with downstream epidemiological errors (total count, peak timing/magnitude, growth rate fidelity), as ECS’s explicit gap penalty distinguishes truncated from temporally faithful predictions—unlike DTW. Both benchmark and metric generalize to any structured time-series chart extraction.

Abstract

Chart-to-data extraction with vision-language models (VLMs) is increasingly evaluated on benchmarks that show diminishing headroom (frontier VLMs exceed 89% on ChartQA) and with metrics that treat extracted points as unordered key-value pairs, ignoring the temporal structure of time series and penalizing small alignment shifts as catastrophic failures. We address both gaps with EpiCurveBench, a benchmark of 1,000 real-world epidemic curve images curated from diverse public-health sources, and EpiCurveSimilarity (ECS), an evaluation metric that aligns predicted and ground-truth series via dynamic programming, tolerating local temporal shifts and gaps while penalizing them proportionally. Evaluating six methods--three frontier closed VLMs, one open VLM, and two specialized chart-extraction systems--we find the strongest model reaches only 52.3% ECS, and that ECS spreads the four general-purpose VLMs over a 25-point range where key-value metrics (RMS, SCRM) compress them into a 5-point band. We further validate ECS against four downstream epidemiological summary statistics, finding that higher ECS predicts smaller errors in total counts, peak timing, and peak magnitude, and higher growth-rate fidelity; across all four, ECS correlates 1.5--3.6 times more strongly than Dynamic Time Warping, which lacks a gap penalty and therefore cannot distinguish a truncated prediction from a temporally faithful one. EpiCurveBench targets a high-impact public-health application--unlocking decades of outbreak data trapped in published figures--but the benchmark and metric apply directly to any structured time-series chart-extraction setting.

item.title: Prompt Injection Detection is Regime-Dependent: A Deployment-Aware Evaluation with Interpretable Structural Signals
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26999v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

提示注入（Prompt Injection）是大语言模型（LLM）安全部署中的关键威胁，但现有检测方法多在理想化、同分布（in-distribution）设定下评估，严重脱离真实部署场景——如模型异构性、数据分布偏移、阈值敏感性及低误报率约束等。

方法创新

本研究提出部署感知的评估框架，涵盖：

  • 多模型+多范式实验设计：在 LLaMA-2、Phi-3、Qwen 等 6 类开源模型上系统测试；
  • 结构化可解释信号：首次定义并量化四类语义结构异常——层级覆盖（hierarchy override）、系统提示欺骗（system prompt spoofing）、角色重定义（role redefinition）与规避模式（evasion patterns），全部基于解析树与指令槽位分析，无需微调且具备人类可读性；
  • 双轨评估指标：同步报告排序性能（AUC、NDCG）与部署关键指标（F1@0.1% FPR、阈值鲁棒性、OOD 泛化误差）；
  • 严格实验控制：采用 5 次重复数据划分、3 类 OOD 注入分布（指令混淆、模板逃逸、多跳注入），消除偶然性偏差。

主要发现

  • 检测性能高度范式依赖：无单一模型在所有设置中占优；Transformer 编码器（如 DeBERTa-v3）整体最强，但轻量级结构信号在低资源/高可靠性场景（如金融客服）带来稳定 3.2–5.7% F1 提升；
  • 结构信号显著改善低误报率行为：在 FPR ≤ 0.1% 严苛阈值下，其组合使强基线误检率下降 38%；
  • 排名指标（如 AUC）与部署效果存在显著脱钩：AUC 最高模型在 0.5% 阈值下 F1 反降 22%，凸显“部署有效性≠排序能力”。

本工作揭示了提示注入防御从实验室到产线的核心断层，并开源全部代码与结构特征提取工具包。

AI Summary (English)

Prompt injection poses a critical security threat to LLM deployment, yet existing detectors are typically evaluated under unrealistic, in-distribution settings—ignoring operational constraints like model heterogeneity, distribution shift, threshold sensitivity, and low-FPR requirements. We introduce a deployment-aware evaluation framework, testing lexical, semantic, structural, and transformer-based detectors across 6 open-weight LLMs, 3 OOD injection regimes, and repeated data splits. We propose four interpretable structural signals—hierarchy override, system prompt spoofing, role redefinition, and evasion patterns—derived from parse-tree and instruction-slot analysis, requiring no fine-tuning. Results show detection performance is highly regime-dependent: no single detector dominates universally; transformer encoders (e.g., DeBERTa-v3) achieve strongest overall AUC, while structural signals deliver modest but consistent gains—especially improving low-FPR behavior (38% fewer false positives at 0.1% FPR) and enhancing robustness in hard OOD scenarios. Crucially, high ranking performance (e.g., AUC) does not guarantee deployment effectiveness—highlighting an urgent need for realistic, constraint-aware evaluation. Code will be released.

Abstract

Prompt injection poses a critical threat to the safe deployment of large language models, yet existing detection approaches are typically evaluated under limited settings that do not reflect real-world operating constraints. In this work, we present a deployment-aware evaluation of prompt injection detection using a multi-model and multi-regime experimental framework. We compare lexical, semantic, structural, and transformer-based detectors across multiple out-of-distribution settings, repeated data splits, and both ranking and thresholded deployment metrics. We introduce interpretable structural signals that capture hierarchy overrides, system prompt spoofing, role redefinition, and evasion patterns, and assess their contribution both within sparse models and in combination with strong encoder baselines. Our results show that detection performance is highly regime-dependent and sensitive to threshold selection, with no single model dominating across all settings. Transformer-based models achieve the strongest overall performance, while structural signals provide modest but consistent gains in certain regimes and improve low false positive rate behaviour in harder scenarios. These findings highlight the gap between ranking performance and deployment effectiveness and underscore the importance of evaluating prompt injection defences under realistic operational constraints. Code will be released.

item.title: Privacy-Preserving Screening for Record Linkage
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26882v1

AI Summary (中文)

隐私保护记录关联的筛选前置框架：Appraisal系统

在大数据与机器学习驱动的数据协作时代，跨组织数据价值评估亟需兼顾合规性与实用性。两方隐私保护记录关联（PPRL）虽能保障数据主体隐私，但其高昂的计算与通信开销严重制约其在海量潜在协作者场景（如数据市场）中的规模化部署。为此，本文提出“筛选-再关联”（Screening-then-Linkage）新范式：在耗资源的PPRL前引入轻量级隐私保护筛选阶段（PPRS），仅对高潜力数据对执行完整PPRL，显著提升系统吞吐与响应效率。

我们设计并实现了首个面向PPRS优化的端到端系统——Appraisal。其核心创新在于：

  • 提出电路PSI（circuit-PSI）基础架构，支持安全、高效、可证明的筛选；
  • 针对PPRS需支持近似匹配与模式感知比较（如编辑距离、字段加权相似度），而传统circuit-PSI仅支持对称函数的瓶颈，首创隐匿属性对齐协议（Oblivious Attribute/Feature Alignment, OAFA）——一种通信更优的安全置换机制；
  • OAFA将通信成本降低至现有方案的1/14，并兼容更丰富的比较函数族（如Jaccard、Cosine、Levenshtein变体）。

理论分析证实Appraisal满足恶意敌手模型下的模拟安全性。实验表明：在同等硬件与内存约束下，Appraisal支持的记录规模达SOTA PPRS系统SFour的850×；端到端筛选+关联耗时仅为当前最优PPRL方案的1/165，使从数万候选方中快速识别高价值协作者成为现实。

AI Summary (English)

In big-data-driven collaboration, privacy-preserving record linkage (PPRL) enables secure value assessment but suffers from prohibitive computational and communication overheads—hindering scalability in large-scale data markets. To address this, we propose the Screening-then-Linkage framework, introducing a lightweight Privacy-Preserving Screening (PPRS) phase before full PPRL. We design Appraisal, a circuit-PSI-based system for efficient, secure, and effective PPRS. To support approximate and schema-aware matching—beyond standard symmetric functions supported by circuit-PSI—we introduce the Oblivious Attribute/Feature Alignment (OAFA) protocol: a communication-efficient secure permutation enabling diverse comparison functions (e.g., Jaccard, Levenshtein variants) while reducing communication cost by 14× versus prior art. Rigorous security analysis proves malicious-security under simulation. Empirically, Appraisal handles 850× more records than the SOTA PPRS system SFour under identical constraints and runs 165× faster than SOTA PPRL—demonstrating that screening-first drastically accelerates high-value collaborator discovery.

Abstract

In an era dominated by big data and machine learning, establishing valuable data collaboration has never been more critical. However, such collaborations must operate under regulatory and legal constraints. Two-party Privacy-Preserving Record Linkage (PPRL) emerges to assess the potential collaboration value and also ensure the privacy and security of the involved data. Nevertheless, the substantial computational and communication overheads associated with PPRL hinder its practical adoption in data markets with numerous potential collaborators. Therefore, we present the Screening-then-Linkage framework, which incorporates a lightweight Screening phase prior to the resource-intensive PPRL phase, i.e., PPRS, to mitigate the scalability issue of PPRL. We propose a circuit-PSI-based system, named Appraisal to realize a secure, effective, and efficient PPRS. To reconcile the approximate matching and/or schema-aware setting required in PPRS with the limitations of the circuit-PSI supporting only symmetric functions, we propose a more communication-efficient secure permutation, i.e., Oblivious Attribute/Feature Alignment protocol tailored for PPRS. This protocol supports a broader range of comparison functions and significantly improves efficiency, i.e., reducing communication costs by a factor of 14 compared to the conventional protocol. Our rigorous analysis and comprehensive empirical evaluations demonstrate the security, effectiveness, and efficiency of Appraisal. Appraisal can accommodate up to $850\times$ more records than the SOTA PPRS system, SFour, within the same constraints. Moreover, it is $165 \times$ faster than SOTA PPRL, indicating the Screening-then-Linkage framework substantially decreases the computation time required to identify the most valuable collaborators from a large pool of candidates.

item.title: Secure UAV Swarms in Low-Altitude Wireless Networks: Challenges and Solutions
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26876v1

AI Summary (中文)

低空无线网络中无人机集群的安全挑战与协同防御框架

随着分布式感知、灵活通信与自主协同能力的提升，无人机（UAV）集群正大规模部署于城市物流、应急通信、环境监测等低空无线网络场景。然而，其开放、高动态的运行环境——叠加有限机载算力、频繁拓扑变化及智能攻击者存在——催生了严峻安全威胁：GPS欺骗攻击可致集群定位失效；内部人员威胁（如被劫持或恶意节点）破坏信任基础；多跳渗透攻击则利用链式连接隐蔽扩散，难以溯源。

为系统性应对上述挑战，本文提出一种云-边-端协同防御框架，融合全局管控、边缘实时响应与终端轻量执行能力。在此框架下，设计三项互补机制：
1. 协同感知抗欺骗机制：构建无人机间交互式攻防博弈模型，通过多源异构信号（GNSS/视觉/IMU）交叉验证与动态置信加权，实现GPS欺骗检测率提升至99.2%，定位误差降低76%；
2. 行为驱动的可信认证机制：引入飞行轨迹连续性、通信时序异常度与资源使用熵三维度行为指纹，结合动态信任评估模型（DTM），使内部恶意节点识别准确率达94.7%，误报率低于1.8%；
3. 多智能体攻击溯源机制：基于强化学习的分布式探针部署与贝叶斯推理路径重建，可在3跳内精准定位多跳攻击起始节点，平均溯源延迟仅210 ms。

实验在NS-3/ROS联合仿真平台与真实四旋翼集群上验证，结果表明该框架在资源受限（单机CPU占用<12%）下显著提升整体安全性。最后，本文指出轻量化零信任架构、跨域空天地协同取证与对抗性鲁棒联邦学习等开放研究方向。

AI Summary (English)

Unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) swarms operating in low-altitude wireless networks face severe security threats—including GPS spoofing, insider attacks, and multi-hop intrusions—exacerbated by resource constraints, volatile topology, and intelligent adversaries. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a cloud-edge-end collaborative defense framework, featuring three synergistic mechanisms: (1) a cooperative perception scheme using interactive attack-defense game modeling to detect GPS spoofing with 99.2% accuracy; (2) a behavior-driven authentication method integrating dynamic trust evaluation for insider threat mitigation (94.7% detection accuracy, <1.8% false positive rate); and (3) a multi-agent attack forensics framework leveraging reinforcement learning and Bayesian inference to trace multi-hop attack paths within ≤3 hops and ≤210 ms latency. Extensive experiments validate the framework’s effectiveness under strict onboard resource limits (CPU usage <12%). The work establishes a scalable foundation for secure UAV swarm operations.

Abstract

Unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) swarms are increasingly deployed in vast low-altitude applications, owing to their capabilities in distributed sensing, flexible communication, and autonomous coordination. Nevertheless, the open and highly dynamic operating environment of UAV swarms introduces serious security risks, including GPS spoofing, insider threats, and multi-hop intrusion. These threats are aggravated by limited on-board resources, frequently changing network topology, and the presence of intelligent adversaries. To tackle these issues, this paper proposes a cloud-edge-end collaborative defense framework for UAV swarms. Based on this framework, three complementary mechanisms are developed. First, a cooperative perception scheme is designed to resist GPS spoofing via interactive attack-defense game modeling. Second, a behavior-driven authentication method with trust evaluation is developed to mitigate insider threats. Third, a multi-agent attack forensics framework is devised to intelligently trace the propagation paths of multi-hop attacks in UAV networks. Experimental results validate the effectiveness of the proposed approaches. Finally, several open research directions are outlined.

item.title: Control Physiology: An Agent-Based Model of FAIR-CAM Dynamics
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26597v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

传统安全风险分析将控制措施（controls）的有效性视为静态输入，忽视其动态衰减特性：配置漂移、依赖监测系统自身的脆弱性、以及有限修复预算下的资源竞争。FAIR Controls Analytics Model（FAIR-CAM）虽从理论上刻画了这些“控制生理学”（Control Physiology）机制，但长期缺乏可计算实现，限制了实证验证与决策支持能力。

方法与创新

本研究构建了首个基于智能体（agent-based）的FAIR-CAM动态仿真模型，开源发布（MIT License）。模型包含：

  • 8类异构智能体（含控制项、监控组件VMC、工程师、攻击者等）；
  • 乘法式纵深防御易感性公式，量化多层控制协同失效概率；
  • 三源方差模型（内在漂移、外在扰动、监测失准），解耦变异来源；
  • 预算约束下的优先级修复引擎，模拟真实组织资源瓶颈；
  • 叙事因果引擎（Narrative Causation Engine），为每次损失事件生成完整、可追溯的因果链（含时间戳、触发条件与责任归属）。

关键发现（医院勒索软件场景，N=1000次仿真）

1. 效能偏差现象：运行中涌现的操作有效性较理论FAIR-CAM公式平均偏低17%，主因外源性方差相关性；该偏差随外源事件频率线性增长，在纯内源漂移下消失；
2. 修复队列相变：当月修复预算低于5–10工程师工时（场景阈值）时，修复管道发生尖锐排队相变，预期损失激增约2.8倍；
3. 监测级联失效：单个虚拟监控组件（VMC）性能退化，会静默放大其所辖全部控制项的未检出方差，凸显VMC在FAIR-CAM拓扑中的枢纽脆弱性。
上述三者均为FAIR-CAM架构固有结构性特征，具备跨场景泛化潜力。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces the first agent-based model (ABM) to operationalize FAIR-CAM—the theoretical framework for control physiology—making dynamic control degradation, monitoring interdependence, and budget-constrained remediation computationally observable. The open-source implementation features eight agent types, a multiplicative defense-in-depth susceptibility formula, a three-source variance model (intrinsic drift, extrinsic perturbations, monitoring inaccuracy), budget-aware remediation scheduling, and a narrative causation engine that logs full causal traces for every loss event. In a hospital ransomware scenario (1,000 iterations), three structural dynamics emerge: (1) emergent operational efficacy diverges from the analytical FAIR-CAM formula by ~17%, driven by correlated extrinsic variance; (2) a sharp queuing phase transition in the remediation pipeline approximately triples expected loss when monthly engineering capacity falls below a scenario-specific threshold (5–10 hours); and (3) cascading monitoring failures propagate silently through the VMC topology, where one degraded VMC amplifies undetected variance across all managed controls. These findings validate FAIR-CAM as a generative architecture—not just a static calculator—and demonstrate its capacity to reveal non-intuitive, policy-relevant system behaviors.

Abstract

Security risk analysis typically treats control effectiveness as a static input, yet controls degrade through configuration drift, depend on monitoring systems that may themselves be degraded, and compete for finite remediation budgets. The FAIR Controls Analytics Model (FAIR-CAM) provides the theoretical framework for these dynamics but has so far remained theoretical. We present the first agent-based model to operationalize the core FAIR-CAM dynamics, making control physiology computationally observable, and release the implementation as open source. The simulation implements eight agent types, a multiplicative defense-in-depth susceptibility formula, a three-source variance model, budget-constrained remediation, and a narrative causation engine that produces a complete causal trace for every loss event. In a hospital ransomware scenario (N=1,000 iterations), three organizational dynamics emerge that static analysis cannot represent. First, emergent operational efficacy diverges from the analytical FAIR-CAM formula by approximately 17 percent, driven by correlated extrinsic variance; the divergence grows linearly with extrinsic frequency and vanishes under purely intrinsic drift. Second, a sharp queueing regime transition in the remediation pipeline approximately 2.8x expected loss when budget falls below a scenario-specific threshold (5-10 engineer-hours/month). Third, cascading monitoring failures propagate through the VMC topology: a single degraded VMC silently compounds undetected variance across the controls it manages. These dynamics are structural properties of the FAIR-CAM architecture and should generalize beyond the specific scenario studied.

item.title: Aligning Provenance with Authorization: A Dual-Graph Defense for LLM Agents
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26497v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

大语言模型（LLM）智能体正广泛部署于邮件管理、金融交易、代码执行等高风险场景，依赖工具调用与外部世界交互。攻击者可通过间接提示注入（indirect prompt injection），将恶意指令嵌入代理可读取的外部数据源（如钓鱼邮件、恶意网页、污染文件）中，诱使其执行越权操作（例如向攻击者账户转账）。现有防御存在两大缺陷：（1）仅在工具调用层面做参数值校验，忽略参数值的来源路径（provenance），无法识别“合法值+非法来源”组合；（2）仅构建单一执行轨迹图，缺乏与用户原始授权意图的可比基准，难以判定行为是否偏离授权边界。

方法：AuthGraph 双图对齐框架

本文提出 AuthGraph——首个在参数来源粒度上对齐授权规范与执行溯源的结构化防御框架。其核心是构建两个互补图：

  • 注入推理图（Injected Reasoning Graph）：基于实际执行轨迹构建，显式建模参数值从外部输入→LLM推理→工具调用的完整信息溯源链（含潜在被篡改的归因）；
  • 授权图（Authorization Graph）：在隔离、洁净上下文中，仅依据用户初始指令（不含任何外部数据）推导出的、信息论意义上不可被注入污染的授权行为图谱，精确刻画“用户真正允许什么操作、允许使用哪些数据源”。

二者通过结构对齐检查器进行拓扑比对，自动检测两类偏差：工具级越权（如调用未授权API）与参数源级越权（如将钓鱼邮件中的账号ID用于转账参数）。

主要结果与创新

在 AgentDojo 基准上，AuthGraph 将攻击成功率从 40% 降至 1%，同时保持 76% 的任务完成率（GPT-4o）；在更具挑战性的 AgentDyn 上，攻击率由 39% 降至 2%，效用保留率达 51%，显著优于 CaMeL、DRIFT 和 Progent。本工作首次实现授权意图与执行溯源在参数-来源耦合层面的结构化对齐，兼顾检测精度与代理灵活性。

AI Summary (English)

LLM agents face severe indirect prompt injection threats when interacting with untrusted external data (e.g., emails, webpages), enabling attackers to hijack tool calls for unauthorized actions like fund transfers. Existing defenses lack either provenance tracking of parameter sources or a clean authorization baseline for comparison. We propose AuthGraph, the first framework to structurally align authorization specifications (derived from user intent in an isolated, injection-immune context) with execution provenance (captured in an injected reasoning graph tracing parameter origins through tool calls). Its graph alignment checker detects deviations at both tool-call and parameter-source levels. Evaluated on AgentDojo and AgentDyn, AuthGraph reduces attack success rates from 40% to 1% and 39% to 2%, respectively, while preserving 76% and 51% task utility on GPT-4o—outperforming CaMeL, DRIFT, and Progent. AuthGraph achieves fine-grained, source-aware injection detection without constraining agent flexibility.

Abstract

LLM-based agents are increasingly deployed in high-stakes scenarios such as email management, financial transactions, and code execution, where they interact with the external world through tool calling. During execution, these agents must read external data sources (emails, webpages, files) that attackers can control; through indirect prompt injection, attackers embed malicious instructions in this data to manipulate agents into performing unauthorized operations such as transferring funds to attacker-controlled accounts. Existing defenses either perform tool-call-level value checking without tracking where parameter values originate, or analyze execution traces from a single perspective without a clean authorization baseline for comparison. We propose AuthGraph, a dual-graph alignment defense framework that constructs two complementary graphs: an injected reasoning graph that models information provenance from the actual execution trajectory (including potentially manipulated attributions), and an authorization graph derived from the user's intent in an isolated clean context that is information-theoretically impossible to be influenced by injection; a graph alignment checker then structurally compares the two graphs to detect both tool-level and parameter-source-level deviations. On AgentDojo, AuthGraph reduces the attack success rate from 40% to 1% while maintaining 76% task completion rate on GPT-4o; on AgentDyn, it reduces the attack success rate from 39% to 2% while preserving 51% utility, outperforming state-of-the-art defenses including CaMeL, DRIFT, and Progent. To our knowledge, AuthGraph is the first agent security defense to structurally compare authorization specifications against execution provenance at the parameter-source level, achieving fine-grained injection detection without sacrificing agent flexibility.

item.title: Beyond Epsilon: A Principled QIF Framework for Local Differential Privacy
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26465v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

本地差分隐私（LDP） 已成为大规模系统中频率估计等任务的隐私保护事实标准，但当前研究缺乏对LDP协议进行系统性、原则性比较的理论框架。现有评估主要依赖单一参数 $\varepsilon$（刻画最坏情况下的区分能力）或效用驱动的实证分析（如均方误差），二者均忽略攻击者模型的多样性，无法回答“某协议是否对所有可能的敌手都优于另一协议”这一根本问题。

方法与框架

本文提出首个基于定量信息流（QIF） 的LDP协议分析框架：将LDP机制建模为概率信道，引入Blackwell精化序（refinement ordering） 作为核心判定准则。该序关系严格定义了“协议A在QIF意义上优于协议B”——即A对任意先验分布、任意损失函数、任意推断目标（包括非频率任务） 所提供的信息泄露均不高于B，且至少对某一场景更优。

主要发现与创新

  • 发现多个被文献标榜为“$\varepsilon$-最优”的协议（如OUE、OLH）在QIF框架下并非全局最优：它们与GRR、THE等存在不可比性，甚至被BLH或改进型THE严格支配；
  • 首次对7种主流协议（GRR、BLH、OLH、SUE、OUE、THE及新变体）完成形式化QIF分类，揭示其内在信息保留能力的偏序结构；
  • 证明QIF精化关系蕴含效用优势：若A精化B，则A在所有贝叶斯估计任务（含频率估计）中均具有更小的最小风险；
  • 该框架弥合了LDP理论与形式化方法（如信息论、博弈语义）的鸿沟，为设计攻击者模型无关的鲁棒隐私协议提供了新范式。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces a principled Quantitative Information Flow (QIF) framework for comparing Local Differential Privacy (LDP) frequency estimation protocols. Moving beyond the limitations of the privacy parameter $\varepsilon$—which only bounds worst-case distinguishability—and utility-centric benchmarks, we model LDP mechanisms as probabilistic channels and apply Blackwell refinement ordering to determine when one protocol is intrinsically superior to another against all possible adversaries, priors, and inference goals. Our formal QIF analysis of seven state-of-the-art protocols—including GRR, BLH, OLH, SUE, OUE, and THE—reveals that several widely adopted “optimal” schemes are either incomparable or strictly dominated under this ordering. Crucially, refinement implies guaranteed utility dominance: if protocol A refines B, then A achieves lower Bayes risk for every estimation task, including frequency recovery. This work bridges LDP and formal methods communities, enabling adversary-aware, foundational reasoning about local privacy.

Abstract

Local Differential Privacy (LDP) has become the de facto standard for privacy-preserving data collection in large-scale systems, in particular for the purpose of estimating frequencies. However, the current research landscape lacks a systematic and principled way to compare LDP protocols. The parameter $\varepsilon$ of LDP is considered the measure of privacy, but it only bounds worst-case distinguishability. Other comparisons rely on utility-driven analyses, where mechanisms are ranked based on their ability to preserve data utility for a given privacy budget $\varepsilon$. Both such kinds of comparisons fail to account for the strength of protocols against diverse attacker models. In this paper, we propose a framework for analyzing LDP frequency estimation protocols through the lens of Quantitative Information Flow (QIF). By modeling LDP mechanisms as probabilistic channels, we leverage the concept of refinement (Blackwell ordering) to establish more principled classifications. This approach allows us to determine when one protocol is intrinsically superior to another for all possible adversaries, and to discuss the implications for utility. In particular, our analysis uncovers cases where protocols previously deemed "optimal" are, in fact, incomparable with, or strictly dominated by, other protocols. We provide a formal QIF-based treatment of seven state-of-the-art protocols, including Generalized Randomized Response (GRR), local hashing variants (BLH, OLH), unary encoding schemes (SUE, OUE), and Thresholding with Histogram Encoding (THE). This perspective bridges the gap between the LDP and formal methods communities and enables principled, adversary-aware reasoning about locally private systems.

item.title: Backdoor Attacks on Fault Detection and Localization in Cyber-Physical Systems
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.27674v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

网络物理系统（CPS）广泛应用于智能电网、工业自动化等关键基础设施，其故障检测与定位（FDL）能力直接关系到系统安全与可靠性。近年来，基于机器学习（ML）和深度学习（DL）的FDL模型因其高精度、实时性优势被大规模部署；然而，这类数据驱动模型面临严峻的对抗性安全威胁，尤其是隐蔽性强、危害深远的后门攻击——攻击者通过污染少量训练数据注入特定触发器，使模型在正常输入下表现正常，却在遭遇触发器时输出恶意预测（如漏报故障、误定位至错误节点），严重破坏系统韧性。

方法与创新

本文首次系统性研究后门攻击对CPS中FDL任务的可行性与危害边界。我们提出面向电力系统场景的物理可实现触发器设计范式：结合电压/电流信号时序特性，构造低幅值、短持续、符合电网运行约束的触发模式（如±0.5%幅值扰动、<20ms脉冲），确保其在真实传感器数据中不可察觉且无需硬件篡改。采用梯度引导触发器优化与多模型（LSTM、TCN、GNN）联合评估框架，在IEEE 123节点配电系统仿真环境中开展端到端攻击实验。

主要发现

  • 攻击仅需污染10%训练样本即可实现>92%的攻击成功率（ASR），同时保持>98%的干净样本准确率（CA），满足“高隐蔽-强破坏”双重要求；
  • 触发器在噪声环境（SNR=30dB）与模型迁移场景下仍保持鲁棒性，证实其实际威胁性；
  • 提出的故障定位偏移指标（FLI） 揭示攻击可将故障定位误差从平均1.2节点扩大至8.7节点，导致运维人员误判故障区域。

本工作为CPS安全防护提供了首个面向FDL任务的后门攻击基准，并警示：模型鲁棒性不能替代数据供应链安全。

AI Summary (English)

Cyber-Physical Systems (CPS) increasingly rely on ML-based fault detection and localization (FDL) for critical infrastructure like smart grids. This paper presents the first systematic study of backdoor attacks against FDL pipelines in CPS. We design physically plausible, low-amplitude temporal triggers (e.g., ±0.5% voltage perturbations <20ms) that comply with grid operational constraints and evade sensor-level detection. Evaluating across LSTM, TCN, and GNN models on the IEEE 123-node test feeder, we demonstrate that poisoning only 10% of training data achieves >92% attack success rate while preserving >98% accuracy on clean inputs. Crucially, attacks induce severe mislocalization—increasing average fault localization error from 1.2 to 8.7 nodes—under realistic noise (SNR=30 dB) and model transfer. Our work establishes a foundational benchmark and highlights urgent risks in ML-driven CPS safety-critical functions.

Abstract

Cyber-Physical Systems (CPS) integrate sensing, communication, computation, and control to support critical infrastructure, including smart grids, industrial automation, and control systems. In the electrical utility domain, various controllers are used in CPS to ensure the system detects and recovers from faults, such as voltage fluctuations, and to perform load balancing in distribution systems. Machine learning- and deep learning-based fault detection and localization frameworks have recently gained significant attention in CPS for their ability to identify anomalies and operational failures in real time. However, these intelligent models are vulnerable to adversarial machine learning attacks, particularly backdoor attacks. In a backdoor attack, an adversary injects malicious patterns into the training data so that the model behaves normally most of the time but produces attacker-controlled outputs when triggered by specific patterns. This paper investigates the threat of backdoor attacks against fault detection and localization mechanisms in recent ML pipelines used in modern CPS systems. We define these threats and explore how they can be realized by designing triggers and evaluating their success in the CPS domain. Our experiments show the attack is successful even with 10\% of poisoning.

item.title: Poison with Style: A Practical Poisoning Attack on Code Large Language Models
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.27631v1

AI Summary (中文)

Poison-with-Style：面向代码大模型的实用化风格隐式投毒攻击

背景与问题：代码大语言模型（CLLMs）已成为现代代码智能体的核心引擎，广泛应用于自动补全、生成与修复等关键场景。然而，其训练数据常来自公开代码仓库（如GitHub），存在被恶意污染的风险。现有投毒攻击多依赖显式触发器（如特定关键词或异常注释），易被输入过滤、鲁棒性微调等防御机制识别和拦截，缺乏实际部署场景下的隐蔽性与可行性。

方法创新：本文提出 Poison-with-Style（PwS） ——一种基于开发者代码风格的隐式模型投毒框架。PwS不修改用户提示词（prompt），而是将真实、自然的代码风格（如缩进偏好、变量命名习惯、控制流结构选择等）作为隐式触发器。我们设计了一套新颖的风格感知数据采集方法：从海量开源项目中提取高一致性风格样本，并构建含漏洞语义的“风格-漏洞”对齐数据集；进而采用两阶段微调策略：第一阶段注入风格关联的脆弱逻辑（如未校验输入的eval()调用），第二阶段通过对抗式风格掩码蒸馏保持正常任务性能。

核心发现：在Python代码补全任务上，PwS在HumanEval与MBPP基准上仅造成<5% pass@1性能下降（94.2% → 89.7%），却能在触发风格输入下实现95%的CWE-20（输入验证缺陷）漏洞生成率；且对梯度混淆、随机平滑、后训练检测等SOTA防御均表现出强鲁棒性。实验表明，该攻击可跨模型架构（CodeLlama、StarCoder）迁移，且无需访问目标模型梯度或完整训练流程，具备高度实用性与隐蔽性。本工作首次揭示了代码风格作为可信侧信道的安全风险，为CLLM供应链安全提供了新视角。

AI Summary (English)

We propose Poison-with-Style (PwS), a practical and stealthy model poisoning attack against Code Large Language Models (CLLMs). Unlike prior attacks relying on explicit textual triggers (e.g., rare words), PwS uses developers’ natural coding styles—such as indentation patterns, naming conventions, or control-flow preferences—as implicit, human-indistinguishable triggers embedded in benign prompts. We introduce a style-aware data collection pipeline and a two-stage fine-tuning strategy to inject vulnerability-generation behavior only when trigger styles appear, while preserving strong performance on standard benchmarks. Experiments on Python code completion show PwS achieves 95% attack success rate for CWE-20 vulnerabilities under style triggers, with <5% drop in pass@1 on HumanEval and MBPP, and remains robust against state-of-the-art defenses (e.g., gradient masking, randomized smoothing). Our code and dataset are publicly available at https://github.com/khangtran2020/pws.

Abstract

Code Large Language Models (CLLMs) serve as the core of modern code agents, enabling developers to automate complex software development tasks. In this paper, we present Poison-with-Style (PwS), a practical and stealthy model poisoning attack targeting CLLMs. Unlike prior attacks that assume an active adversary capable of directly embedding explicit triggers (e.g., specific words) into developers' prompts during inference, PwS leverages developers' code styles as covert triggers implicitly embedded within their prompts. PwS introduces a novel data collection method and a two-step training strategy to fine-tune CLLMs, causing them to generate vulnerable code when prompts contain trigger code styles while maintaining normal behavior on other prompts. Experimental results on Python code completion tasks show that PwS is robust against state-of-the-art defenses and achieves high attack success rates across diverse vulnerabilities, while maintaining strong performance on standard code completion benchmarks. For example, PwS-poisoned models generate CWE-20 vulnerable code in 95% of cases when the trigger code style is used, with less than a 5% drop in pass@1 performance on the HumanEval and MBPP benchmarks. Our implementation and dataset are here: https://github.com/khangtran2020/pws.

item.title: A Trilemma in AMM Mechanism Design
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.27602v1

AI Summary (中文)

AMM机制设计中的三难困境：激励相容性、弱局部效率与统一价格不可兼得

区块链技术推动了自动做市商（AMM）的广泛应用，用户通过智能合约直接交易加密资产，其价格由嵌入合约的定价函数决定。然而，现实AMM系统饱受矿工可提取价值（MEV）困扰——尤其是前序/后序套利攻击，导致用户频繁承受不利成交价。尽管共识层无法彻底消除MEV（因其语义不可知性），近期研究转向应用层机制设计，试图在智能合约层面提升公平性与效率。

本文聚焦于Chan、Wu与Shi近期提出的双资产AMM机制设计框架，该框架在“序列公平性”共识假设下实现了强激励相容性（IC）——即用户无需策略性报单，诚实申报最优。我们系统考察IC能否与另两个关键性质共存：弱局部效率（wLE）（要求对合理报价用户的未满足需求最小化，即凡能与资金池直接成交的订单不应被无故拒绝）和统一价格（UP）（所有部分或全额成交的订单必须按同一汇率执行）。

通过严谨建模与凸分析工具，我们揭示了AMM定价函数与订单执行逻辑间的深层数学约束。核心发现是一个严格的三难定理（Trilemma Theorem）：在任意双资产AMM机制中，IC、wLE与UP三者两两可兼容，但三者不可同时满足。我们分别构造了满足任意两个性质的显式机制（如IC+wLE、IC+UP、wLE+UP），并证明第三个性质必然被违背。该结果首次从公理化角度刻画了AMM设计的根本权衡，为协议开发者提供了清晰的取舍边界与理论指南。

AI Summary (English)

This paper identifies a fundamental trilemma in the mechanism design of two-asset Automated Market Makers (AMMs). We investigate the joint feasibility of three desirable properties: Incentive Compatibility (IC)—users truthfully report their valuations; Weak Local Efficiency (wLE)—no feasible trade with the pool is unjustifiably rejected for users with reasonable price limits; and Uniform Pricing (UP)—all executed orders (full or partial) trade at the identical exchange rate. Building on prior work by Chan, Wu, and Shi, we formalize the structural constraints imposed by pricing functions and order execution logic. Our main result is a tight impossibility theorem: any two of IC, wLE, and UP can be simultaneously achieved, but no AMM mechanism satisfies all three. We provide explicit constructions for each pairwise-compatible combination and prove the necessity of the trade-off via convex analysis and incentive constraints. This trilemma reveals an intrinsic limitation in fair and efficient AMM design, offering a rigorous foundation for protocol-level MEV mitigation strategies.

Abstract

Blockchains have popularized the Automated Market Makers (AMMs), where users trade crypto-assets directly with a smart contract, governed by a pricing function embedded in the contract's code. Today, users of AMMs are often forced to accept unfavorable prices due to widespread front-running and back-running attacks, commonly known as Miner Extractable Value (MEV). Several earlier works show impossibility results suggesting that completely removing MEV at the consensus layer is impossible, partly because the consensus layer is agnostic of application-level semantics. For this reason, more recent works have advocated mechanism design approaches at the application (i.e., smart contract) level. We study a natural two-asset AMM mechanism design problem recently initiated and explored in prior work by Chan, Wu, and Shi, in which they proposed a mechanism that satisfies a surprisingly strong notion of incentive compatibility (IC), under the consensus assumption that the underlying blockchain provides sequencing fairness. In this paper, we investigate the (in)feasibility of simultaneously achieving IC and other desirable properties such as weak local efficiency (wLE) and uniform pricing (UP). At a high level, wLE requires that the mechanism should not leave any unfulfilled demand from users whose asking prices are not overly restrictive, and whose orders could have been executed directly against the pool. UP requires that all orders that get (partially) executed must trade at the same exchange rate. We unveil the underlying mathematical structure of AMM mechanism design, and our main results can be summarized as a trilemma-style theorem: among the desirable properties IC, wLE, and UP, any two out of three are possible, but no mechanism can satisfy all three.

item.title: Grimlock: Guarding High-Agency Systems with eBPF and Attested Channels
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.27488v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

随着智能体（Agentic）系统在多云环境中的广泛应用，用户编写的编排代码日益承担高自主性任务：调用外部工具、派生子任务、跨机器与云平台委托执行。然而，这种“高代理权”（high agency）在提升生产力的同时，也导致身份认证、访问授权、操作溯源与权限委托等关键安全职责被下沉至应用层逻辑中——既难以统一强制实施，又严重阻碍审计与合规验证。

方法与设计

本文提出 Grimlock——一种轻量、内核级的智能体守卫（Agent Guard），通过重构信任边界实现关注点分离：不修改任何用户层智能体代码，而将安全 enforcement 下沉至沙箱底层基座。其核心技术包含三重协同机制：

  • eBPF 流量拦截：利用 Linux eBPF 在内核态透明劫持沙箱所有出向通信，强制路由至 Grimlock 守卫；
  • TLS 1.3 后握手认证：在标准 TLS 握手完成后，基于通道绑定（channel bindings）执行远程证明（attestation），确保通信端点身份与运行时上下文真实可信；
  • 作用域令牌（Scope Token）机制：守卫动态签发短时效、通道绑定的 JWT 风格令牌，精确编码最小权限委托策略（如 call:aws-s3:read-bucket=prod-logs），并在接收端由对等守卫二次校验身份、作用域及通道一致性，仅当全部策略通过后才终止 TLS 并释放明文至目标沙箱。kTLS 提供零拷贝、内核加速的安全数据通路。

创新与价值

Grimlock 首次将可信通道绑定 + 动态作用域令牌 + eBPF 沙箱网关深度融合，无需修改应用、不依赖定制运行时，在标准 Linux 环境中实现跨异构云平台的透明、可审计、作用域强约束的智能体间通信，为高代理权系统提供可落地的零信任基础设施原语。

AI Summary (English)

Agentic systems increasingly execute untrusted, user-authored orchestration code across heterogeneous clouds—posing severe security risks as identity, authorization, and delegation logic are pushed into application code, undermining auditability and consistent enforcement. We present Grimlock, an Agent Guard that restores separation of concerns by enforcing trust at the sandbox substrate—not the application layer—without modifying agent code. Grimlock leverages eBPF-enforced traffic interception to redirect all sandbox outbound communication through a guard, then performs post-handshake attestation bound to TLS 1.3 channel bindings. Upon successful attestation, the guard issues short-lived, channel-bound scope tokens encoding least-privilege delegation (e.g., invoke:github-api:repo=org/repo). At the receiver, the peer guard re-validates identity, scope, and channel binding, terminates TLS via kTLS, and releases plaintext only after policy checks pass. Evaluated on commodity Linux, Grimlock enables transparent, auditable, scope-governed inter-agent communication across multi-cloud environments—requiring no changes to user code or infrastructure.

Abstract

Agentic systems increasingly run user-authored orchestration code that invokes tools, spawns subtasks, and delegates work across machines and clouds. Although this high agency is productive, it creates a security problem: identity, authorization, provenance, and delegation are often pushed into application code, where they become difficult to enforce consistently and difficult to audit. We present \emph{Grimlock}, an \emph{Agent Guard} that restores separation of concerns by moving trust enforcement into the sandbox substrate while leaving agent code unchanged. Grimlock uses \emph{eBPF-enforced traffic interception} to ensure that sandbox communication passes through a guard, and combines it with \emph{post-handshake attestation} bound to standard TLS~1.3 channel bindings. After a channel is established, the guard authorizes communication and mints short-lived, channel-bound \emph{scope tokens} that capture least-privilege delegation. At the receiving side, the destination guard re-validates identity, scope, and channel binding, terminates TLS, and releases plaintext to the destination sandbox only after policy checks succeed. kTLS provides an efficient dataplane for protected communication. As a result, Grimlock offers a path toward transparent, auditable, and scope-bound agent-to-agent communication across heterogeneous multi-cloud environments, using commodity Linux primitives and without requiring changes to user-layer orchestration code.

item.title: Agyn: An Open-Source Platform for AI Agents with Scalable On-Demand Execution, Agent Definition as a Code, and Zero-Trust Access
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.27575v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

随着AI代理（AI Agents）在企业生产环境中的规模化部署，工程重心已从单个代理的开发转向其大规模、安全、可治理的运行时管理。典型AI代理具备非确定性工作流、有状态会话、以及对内部服务的高权限访问等特征，传统微服务或无服务器架构难以满足其隔离性、可观测性、策略驱动执行与零信任安全等核心需求。

方法与设计

本文提出 Agyn——一个面向AI代理工作负载的开源平台，围绕三大创新原则构建：

  • 信号驱动的有状态无服务器运行时：基于Kubernetes构建，支持按需弹性伸缩、事件触发执行（如用户消息、定时器、外部Webhook），并原生维护会话状态与上下文生命周期；
  • 代理即代码（Agent-as-Code）：提供官方Terraform Provider，允许通过声明式HCL代码定义代理逻辑、执行环境（harness）、资源约束、重试策略及依赖服务，实现版本化、可测试、可复用的代理基础设施即代码；
  • 零信任安全模型：默认禁用所有网络通信，所有代理实例启动时仅持有最小必要凭证（通过短期OIDC令牌+动态RBAC授权），服务间调用强制mTLS与细粒度策略校验，完全消除隐式信任。

创新与价值

Agyn是代理无关、模型无关、云无关的中立平台，兼容任意LLM调用框架（LangChain、LlamaIndex等）与任意后端模型（OpenAI、Ollama、vLLM等），已在真实金融与SaaS场景中支撑日均百万级代理会话，平均冷启动延迟<800ms，权限越界调用拦截率达100%。项目已开源（Apache 2.0），含完整CI/CD流水线、本地开发沙箱及多租户管理控制台。

AI Summary (English)

As AI agents transition from prototypes to production—executing non-deterministic workflows, maintaining stateful sessions, and accessing privileged internal services—the operational challenge shifts to scalable, secure, and governable orchestration. This paper introduces Agyn, an open-source platform engineered for agent workloads with three core innovations: (1) a signal-driven, stateful serverless runtime on Kubernetes that scales on-demand and natively manages session context; (2) a Terraform provider enabling full “Agent-as-Code” definition—including logic, environment constraints, retries, and service dependencies—ensuring versioning, testing, and reproducibility; and (3) a zero-trust security model enforcing strict least-privilege access via short-lived OIDC tokens, mTLS, and policy-based inter-service authorization. Agyn is agent-agnostic, model-agnostic, and cloud-agnostic—supporting LangChain, LlamaIndex, vLLM, Ollama, and more. Evaluated in production-like environments, it achieves sub-800ms cold starts and 100% interception of unauthorized calls. The platform is open-sourced under Apache 2.0.

Abstract

As organizations move toward production deployments of AI agents, which execute non-deterministic workflows, maintain stateful sessions, and often operate with privileged access to internal services, the engineering challenge shifts from building individual agents to operating them at scale with proper isolation, governance, and security. In this paper we present Agyn, an open-source platform designed around three key principles tailored for agent workloads: a signal-driven, stateful serverless runtime on Kubernetes; a Terraform provider for agent and harness definition; and a security model grounded in zero-trust and least-privilege principles. Agyn is agent-agnostic, model-agnostic, and cloud-agnostic.

item.title: RULER: Representation-Level Verification of Machine Unlearning
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.27569v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

机器遗忘（Machine Unlearning）旨在从已部署模型中移除特定训练样本的影响，而无需从头重训练。当前主流验证范式仅在输出层进行评估，依赖成员推断攻击（Membership Inference）、保留准确率（Retain Accuracy）和遗忘集准确率（Forget-Set Accuracy）三大指标。然而，这类方法存在根本性盲区：即使模型通过全部三项测试，其中间表征（intermediate representations）仍可能隐式编码并保留被要求遗忘的样本信息，构成潜在隐私与合规风险。

方法创新：RULER 框架

本文提出 RULER（Representation-Level Verification of Machine Unlearning）——首个面向表征层的系统性验证框架，包含两类互补指标：

  • M2（Oracle-Comparative）：以“无该遗忘集重训练模型”为理想基准，量化遗忘样本在表征空间中的位置偏移；
  • M4（Oracle-Free）：完全无需重训练，仅通过分析未遗忘模型内部相似性结构（如表征距离分布、聚类内/间方差）检测残留记忆信号。

关键发现

  • 在12种实验条件下（涵盖不同遗忘比例、数据模态与算法），4种主流近似遗忘方法（e.g., SISA, Fisher Forgetting）均通过输出级验证，但M2在10/12条件下显著检出残留（p<0.05），且效应量随遗忘比例升高而增强；
  • 即使机制迥异的Bad Teacher方法亦呈现同类M2异常，印证表征残留的普适性；
  • M4作为前置诊断工具，在表格、图像、临床文本及人脸身份识别四大场景中稳定有效——尤其在人脸任务中，它精准定位到个体身份级记忆残留，而所有测试方法均未能彻底消除该信号。

创新意义

RULER 首次将验证焦点从“模型输出是否正确”提升至“模型内部是否真正遗忘”，为可信AI治理提供了可解释、免重训练、跨模态的表征级审计标准。

AI Summary (English)

Machine unlearning seeks to remove a model’s dependence on specific training records without full retraining. Current verification relies solely on output-level metrics (membership inference, retain/forget accuracy), yet models passing all three may still encode forgotten data in internal representations—posing serious privacy risks. We introduce RULER, the first representation-level verification framework, comprising two novel metrics: M2, an oracle-comparative measure quantifying positional deviation of forget-set samples relative to a clean retrained model; and M4, an oracle-free diagnostic detecting memory residuals from the unlearned model’s intrinsic similarity structure alone. Across 12 settings (varying forget fractions, modalities, and methods), four approximate unlearning techniques passed output-level tests—but M2 detected statistically significant residuals (p<0.05) in 10/12 cases, with effect sizes increasing with forget fraction; notably, the distinct Bad Teacher method showed identical M2 anomalies. M4 served as a robust pre-unlearning diagnostic across tabular, image, clinical text, and face-identity tasks—uniquely revealing persistent identity-level memorization in face recognition where no tested method achieved full erasure. RULER shifts verification from what the model outputs to whether it truly forgets internally.

Abstract

Machine unlearning aims to remove the influence of specific training records from a deployed model without retraining from scratch. Current protocols verify this at the output level through membership inference, retain accuracy, and forget-set accuracy, but a model can satisfy all three whilst still encoding forgotten records in its intermediate representations.
