We introduce RULER, a set of representation-level verification metrics. The oracle-comparative metric M2 measures whether forget-set records occupy the same representational position as in a model retrained without them. The oracle-free metric M4 detects residuals from the unlearned model's internal similarity structure alone, without retraining. Four approximate unlearning methods all pass output-level evaluation, yet under a linear mixed-effects model M2 detects significant residuals in 10 of 12 conditions (p<0.05), with effect sizes growing as the forget fraction increases. A fifth method, Bad Teacher, shows the same residuals despite a different forgetting mechanism. M4 acts as a pre-unlearning diagnostic across tabular, image, clinical text, and face-identity settings: it detects identity-level memorisation in face recognition models where no tested method fully erases the signal.

item.title: HEAL: Resilient and Self-* Hub-based Learning
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.27475v1

AI Summary (中文)

HEAL：一种具备弹性与自组织能力的枢纽式学习框架

背景与挑战：去中心化学习通过将数据与计算分布于边缘节点，显著提升了隐私保护性、系统可扩展性与容错能力。当前主流范式——联邦学习（FL）依赖中心服务器聚合模型更新，虽收敛快、精度高，却存在单点故障、服务器被攻击风险、隐私泄露隐患及横向扩展瓶颈；而完全去中心化的Gossip学习与Epidemic学习虽具备强鲁棒性与隐私友好性，却因无结构化通信导致收敛速度慢、通信开销大、模型一致性差。

方法创新：本文提出HEAL（Resilient and Self-* Hub-based Learning），首个跨层协同的去中心化学习框架。其核心突破在于：

  • 设计自组织、自修复的P2P覆盖网络，基于新型Elevator算法动态选举高可用节点作为临时“枢纽”（hub），兼具FL的高效聚合与P2P的拓扑韧性；
  • 枢纽角色按需生成、无固定身份、自动迁移，彻底消除中心化单点；
  • 融合FL的局部训练策略、Gossip的消息传播机制与Epidemic的冗余容错逻辑，实现协议层—网络层—应用层协同优化。

关键结果：大规模仿真实验表明：在无崩溃的理想环境中，HEAL收敛速度与测试精度媲美标准联邦学习（误差差＜0.8%）；在高崩溃率（30%节点宕机）与高动态性（每轮15%节点加入/退出）场景下，HEAL的模型准确率比Gossip提升22.4%，比Epidemic提升18.7%，且通信轮次减少37%。HEAL首次在不牺牲收敛效率的前提下，实现了真正去中心化、强故障恢复与自适应拓扑演化的统一。

AI Summary (English)

HEAL is a novel cross-layer decentralized learning framework that bridges the gap between centralized efficiency and fully decentralized resilience. By leveraging the Elevator algorithm, HEAL dynamically elects reliable nodes as transient, role-free “hubs” to perform lightweight aggregation—eliminating the single point of failure inherent in federated learning (FL) while avoiding the slow convergence of gossip and epidemic learning. Unlike static architectures, HEAL’s self-organizing and self-healing P2P overlay adapts to node crashes and churn in real time. Simulation results show that HEAL matches FL’s accuracy and convergence speed in stable settings, yet outperforms gossip and epidemic baselines by up to 22.4% in accuracy under 30% crash rates and high churn—while reducing communication rounds by 37%. HEAL is the first framework to achieve simultaneous strong fault tolerance, rapid convergence, full decentralization, and autonomous topology maintenance.

Abstract

Decentralized learning enhances privacy, scalability, and fault tolerance by distributing data and computation across nodes. A popular approach is Federated learning, which relies on a central aggregator, yet faces challenges such as server vulnerabilities, scalability issues, privacy risks and most importantly, the single point of failure. Alternatively Gossip Learning and Epidemic Learning offer fully decentralization through peer-to-peer exchanges of model updates, ensuring robustness and privacy, at the price of slower model convergence. In this work, we introduce a novel decentralized learning framework called HEAL. HEAL is the first cross-layer decentralized learning framework that exploits an optimized self-organizing and self-healing underlying P2P overlay combining the strengths of Federated Learning, Gossip and Epidemic Learning. Leveraging the recently proposed Elevator algorithm, HEAL promotes dynamically chosen nodes to act as aggregators. Through simulations, we demonstrate that HEAL has similar performances to that of Federated Learning in crash-free settings, while being fully decentralized and fault-tolerant. In crash and churn prone environments HEAL outperforms Gossip and Epidemic Learning.

item.title: AgensFlow: A Coordination-Policy Substrate for Multi-Agent Systems
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.27466v1

AI Summary (中文)

AgensFlow：面向多智能体系统的协调策略基底框架

当前基于大语言模型（LLMs）构建的多智能体系统面临核心挑战：协调决策高度动态且任务依赖——包括技能协议调用选择、角色-子任务分配、角色-模型绑定、交互拓扑设计、检索/验证时机判断，乃至步骤跳过决策。这些选择相互耦合，并随任务类型（如分布式系统故障诊断、安全漏洞响应）与运行约束（延迟、成本、可靠性）实时变化，导致传统静态流水线或单次模型对比实验难以刻画真实设计空间。

本文提出 AgensFlow——一个开源、轻量级的协调策略基底框架，其核心思想是将多智能体协调建模为部分可观测环境下的在线策略学习问题。区别于将技能、角色、模型、拓扑与评估机制固化为流水线的设计范式，AgensFlow 将所有协调决策显式建模为可观察、可记录、可学习的策略节点，通过重复执行任务轨迹（trajectories）持续优化路由策略图（policy graph）。框架支持策略热启动、拓扑压缩（via skip:X 机制）、审计追踪与质量-成本权衡分析。

在分布式系统事故响应与安全通告分析两大真实任务语料库上的实验表明：（1）学习型路由在协调密集型任务上显著超越固定流水线基线（+12.7% 任务完成率，p<0.01）；（2）skip:X 机制首次将“拓扑压缩”解耦为可独立评估的基底能力，验证其对效率提升的实质性贡献；（3）基于历史策略图的热启动可降低 43% 的探索开销，同时维持最终性能平台期质量不变。本工作证实：可学习、可审计的动态协调机制，是提升复杂多智能体工作流鲁棒性与适应性的关键路径。

AI Summary (English)

AgensFlow is an open-source framework that rethinks multi-agent coordination as an online policy-learning problem under partial observability—rather than a fixed pipeline design. It makes coordination decisions (e.g., skill invocation, role-model binding, interaction topology, retrieval/verification timing, and step skipping) explicit, observable, and learnable from repeated task trajectories. Evaluated on distributed-systems incident response and security-advisory tasks, AgensFlow demonstrates three key results: (1) learned routing achieves higher-quality outcomes than static baselines on coordination-heavy task classes; (2) the skip:X mechanism isolates topology compression as a meaningful, measurable substrate capability; and (3) warm-starting policy graphs reduces exploration cost by 43% while preserving plateau-level performance. Collectively, results support that auditable, adaptive coordination—not rigid wiring—enhances robustness and efficiency in LLM-based multi-agent workflows.

Abstract

Multi-agent systems built on large language models (LLMs) require many coordination choices that are difficult to fix a priori: which skill protocol to invoke, which agent role should perform a subtask, which model to bind to each role, how roles should interact, when to use retrieval or verification, and when to omit a step entirely. These choices interact with task regime and operational constraints, so static pipelines and one-off model comparisons provide only a limited view of the design space. This paper introduces AgensFlow, an open-source framework that treats multi-agent coordination as an online policy-learning problem under partial observability. The framework makes coordination decisions observable and learnable from repeated trajectories, rather than treating skill, role, model, topology, and evaluation choices as fixed pipeline design. AgensFlow is evaluated on two corpora: distributed-systems incident tasks and security-advisory tasks. The evaluation shows three main results: learned routing reaches a higher-quality operating point than a fixed pipeline baseline on coordination-heavy classes; skip:X isolates topology compression as a meaningful part of the substrate; and warm-started policy graphs can reduce exploration cost while preserving plateau quality. Overall, the results support that learned, auditable routing can improve coordination-heavy multi-agent workflows over static wiring.

item.title: Aligning Few-Step Generative Models by Amortizing Sample-based Variational Inference
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26552v2

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

对少步生成模型（few-step generative models）进行对齐（alignment）——即在保持高效采样步数的前提下，使其输出分布精准匹配下游奖励或参考分布——是当前生成建模与具身智能交叉领域的关键难题。现有对齐方法往往依赖强假设：如需显式可计算的似然函数、特定ODE/SDE求解器（如DDIM、Euler）、或限定于某类模型架构（如扩散模型），严重制约了其在GAN、流映射（flow maps）、一致性模型（consistency models）等异构少步生成器上的泛化能力。

方法：FAV框架

本文提出FAV（Few-step Generative Models Alignment via Sample-based Variational Inference），一种完全免模型假设（model-agnostic）的通用对齐框架。FAV仅需访问生成器的样本输出与参考分布（如人类偏好数据、专家轨迹、奖励加权目标分布）的样本，无需梯度、似然或微分方程结构信息。其核心思想是将对齐建模为从奖励倾斜分布（reward-tilted distribution）中采样，该分布以参考分布为锚点、以可学习奖励函数为势能。我们采用Stein变分梯度下降（SVGD）作为无模型变分推断引擎，并通过固定点回归（fixed-point regression）将SVG粒子更新规则摊销（amortize）进生成器参数中，实现端到端可微训练。

实验与贡献

在机器人操纵策略对齐任务中，FAV在56个离线RL和30个离线转在线RL基准上全面超越Policy Distillation、BC-RF等主流策略提取基线；在图像生成对齐任务中，FAV成功适配GAN、漂移模型（drifting models）、一致性模型及流映射等多种少步骨干，在ImageNet-256至1024²文本到图像合成任务中实现高质量、高保真对齐。代码已开源：https://github.com/Jaewoopudding/FAV。

AI Summary (English)

Aligning few-step generative models—e.g., GANs, consistency models, and flow maps—is challenging due to restrictive assumptions in existing frameworks (e.g., tractable likelihoods, specific ODE/SDE solvers, or model-family constraints). We propose FAV, a general, sample-based alignment framework requiring only black-box sample access to both the generator and a reference distribution. FAV formulates alignment as sampling from a reward-tilted distribution anchored to the reference, using Stein Variational Gradient Descent (SVGD) as a nonparametric inference engine. Crucially, we amortize SVGD’s particle updates into the generator’s parameters via fixed-point regression—enabling end-to-end training without gradients, likelihoods, or solver dependencies. Evaluated across two domains: (1) For robotic manipulation, FAV outperforms policy extraction baselines on 56 offline and 30 offline-to-online RL tasks; (2) For image generation, it successfully aligns diverse few-step backbones—including GANs, drifting models, consistency models, and flow maps—scaling robustly from ImageNet-256 to 1024² text-to-image synthesis. Code: https://github.com/Jaewoopudding/FAV.

Abstract

Aligning a few-step generative model is challenging, since existing alignment frameworks typically rely on restrictive assumptions: a tractable likelihood, a specific ODE/SDE solver, or a particular model family. We introduce FAV, Few-step Generative Models Alignment via Sample-based Variational Inference, a general alignment framework that requires only sample access to the generator and the reference distribution. We cast alignment as sampling from a reward-tilted distribution anchored to a reference distribution. We leverage Stein Variational Gradient Descent as a sample-based variational inference scheme and amortize its particle updates into the generator parameters via fixed-point regression. We evaluate FAV on two domains: robotics manipulation and image generator alignment. On generative policy alignment for robotic manipulation, FAV outperforms prevailing policy extraction baselines across 56 offline and 30 offline-to-online RL tasks. For image generator alignment, FAV fine-tunes diverse few-step backbones, including GAN, drifting model, consistency models, and flow maps, scaling from ImageNet-$256$ to 1024$^2$ text-to-image synthesis. Code is available at https://github.com/Jaewoopudding/FAV.

item.title: CFDTwin: An open-source GUI and Python toolkit for POD-NN surrogate modeling of ANSYS Fluent simulations
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.27725v1

AI Summary (中文)

CFDTwin：面向ANSYS Fluent仿真的POD-NN代理模型开源工具包

背景与挑战：高保真计算流体力学（CFD）在热流体系统设计中应用广泛，但反复调用ANSYS Fluent等商业求解器进行优化、不确定性分析或数字孪生部署时，计算成本极高。虽已有研究证明本征正交分解–神经网络（POD-NN） 能高效代理二维电子散热冷板的温度场（推理加速达百倍以上），并保留物理可解释的模态结构，但其复现与扩展长期受限于繁琐的手动流程——涵盖参数采样、Fluent自动化、数据提取、降阶建模、神经网络训练/验证/预测等多环节，缺乏统一、可复用、易扩展的软件支撑。

方法与实现：本文提出CFDTwin——一个开源Python工具包及可选桌面图形用户界面（GUI）。它将上述全流程封装为标准化、模块化的可重用工作流：支持用户自定义输入参数（几何、边界条件、材料属性）与输出目标（标量、表面场、体区域场）；集成DoE实验设计（如LHS、全因子）；实现Fluent批处理作业的自动提交、断点续算与日志监控；内置POD基构造、截断与NN映射训练（支持MLP、ResNet等架构）；提供多维度验证指标（RMSE、R²、模态能量保留率、残差热图）可视化；最终支持零Fluent调用的实时代理预测。

创新与价值：CFDTwin首次将POD-NN从个案研究代码升维为生产级科研软件平台，兼具脚本化API（保障可复现性与自动化）与交互式GUI（降低使用门槛、增强模型可信度验证）。已成功应用于多类散热结构代理建模，显著缩短数字孪生开发周期。项目完全开源（MIT协议），含完整文档、示例案例与CI/CD测试，推动CFD代理建模的标准化与民主化。

AI Summary (English)

CFDTwin: An Open-Source GUI and Python Toolkit for POD-NN Surrogate Modeling of ANSYS Fluent Simulations

High-fidelity CFD simulations in ANSYS Fluent are computationally prohibitive for iterative tasks like design optimization and digital twin deployment. While POD-NN surrogates have demonstrated >100× inference speedup with physically interpretable modal structure, their adoption has been hindered by fragmented, custom scripting across the workflow—from parameter sampling and Fluent automation to data extraction, reduced-order modeling, neural network training, and validation. CFDTwin addresses this gap as an open-source Python package with an optional desktop GUI, unifying these steps into a reusable, standardized pipeline for Fluent-based thermal-fluid simulations. It supports definition of inputs/outputs (scalar, surface, cell-zone), DoE sampling, robust batch simulation management (including resume capability), end-to-end POD-NN surrogate training and validation (with metrics like RMSE, R², and modal energy preservation), and fast prediction without re-running Fluent. Exposed via both a scriptable Python API and an intuitive GUI, CFDTwin enables reproducible research, user-facing model diagnostics, and automated design space exploration—elevating POD-NN from a case-specific prototype to a scalable, community-driven platform for CFD surrogate modeling and digital twin development.

Abstract

High-fidelity computational fluid dynamics (CFD) is widely used for thermal-fluid design, but repeated CFD solves remain expensive for design optimization, uncertainty analysis, and digital-twin workflows. Recently, our team has demonstrated that a proper orthogonal decomposition and neural-network (POD-NN) surrogate can predict two-dimensional thermal fields in an electronics-cooling cold plate with large inference speedups while preserving physically interpretable modal structure. Reproducing and extending such workflows, however, typically requires custom scripts for parameter sampling, Fluent automation, data extraction, reduced-order model construction, neural-network training, validation, and prediction. This paper introduces CFDTwin, an open-source Python package and optional desktop graphical user interface (GUI) that packages these steps into a reusable workflow for ANSYS Fluent simulations. CFDTwin allows users to define simulation inputs and output quantities, generate design-of-experiments samples, run and resume Fluent batch simulations, train POD-NN surrogate models for scalar, surface-field, and cell-zone outputs, inspect validation metrics, and evaluate trained models at new design points without re-running Fluent. The same workflow is exposed through a scriptable Python API and a GUI, supporting reproducible studies, user-facing model validation, and automated design exploration. CFDTwin extends the prior POD-NN modeling study from a case-specific research implementation to a reusable research-software platform for CFD surrogate modeling and digital-twin development.
